source.title: Introduce Version Safety Advisor and retrograde-compatibility checks for upgrades/rollbacks
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.release_notes: Add a Version Safety Advisor that runs pre-upgrade and pre-downgrade compatibility checks; introduce timed restore locks to prevent concurrent restores; add Helm hook validation and Terraform module validator; non-breaking default behavior with opt-in enforcement via installer flags.
source.description:
Summary: Adds a new 'Version Safety Advisor' subsystem to the private installer that performs pre-deploy compatibility checks for upgrades and downgrades (retrograde checks), surface actionable incompatibility reports, and optionally block unsafe rollouts. Also introduces a timed restore lock to serialize restore operations, Helm chart validation hooks, and a Terraform module-level validation script.

Motivation: Past incidents (SUP-1342) showed latent schema incompatibilities and concurrent restore races during cluster upgrade windows. The existing installer provided atomic migration scripts but lacked a deterministic preflight that would catch mixing of incompatible manifests, deprecated CRDs, or database schema regressions when attempting a downgrade path. The goal is to reduce upgrade/rollback blast radius by surfacing problems before changes are applied and by preventing concurrent restore operations that could corrupt state.

What changed (high level):
- New component: installer/advisor/version_safety_advisor.go + CLI binding (installer-cli advisor check --from <v> --to <v>) that computes a compatibility score and detailed report for: Helm chart changes, CRD schema diffs, migration direction (upgrade/downgrade), and DB migration plan viability.
- Retrograde compatibility: analyzer detects incompatible DB schema changes that would break a downgrade path and marks them as 'retrograde-risk'.
- Timed restore lock: installer/ops/restore_lock implements a TTL-lock stored in cluster state (ConfigMap with HMAC) and checked by both installer and private-installer restore commands to ensure only one active restore per tenant and to auto-expire if the operator fails mid-restore.
- Helm chart changes: added validation hooks (templates/validation-hook.yaml) that run static checks against target cluster resource quota and required CRDs; added chart annotations to surface minimum compatible controller versions.
- Terraform module validator: scripts/terraform/validate_module_versions.sh that ensure pinned provider/module versions and emit advisory warnings when providers are out-of-date with the target release.
- Integration tests: e2e/advisor/compatibility_test.go simulates a three-release matrix (v1.14 -> v1.16 -> v1.13) to exercise both forward- and backward-compat checks and validates lock behavior under concurrent restores.
- Docs: docs/upgrade-safety-advisor.md with examples and suggested remediation steps when advisor reports 'blocking' issues.

Commits (summary):
- commit 7a3d2c1: scaffold version safety advisor plumbing and CLI command binding
- commit b14f8d9: implement Helm and CRD diffing logic; add unit tests for parser
- commit e9a2c4f: add timed restore lock with HMAC TTL semantics and tests
- commit f2c19b2: add Terraform validator and CI job to lint module pins
- commit 4c8d7e1: integration tests + docs + small chart adjustments

Files changed (high-level examples, not full diffs):
- installer/advisor/version_safety_advisor.go (+480 lines) - main advisor logic and report serializer
- installer/cli/advisor.go (+60 lines) - new CLI command and flags
- templates/validation-hook.yaml (+45 lines) - helm validation hook that runs pre-install checks
- installer/ops/restore_lock.go (+120 lines) - timed restore lock implementation and HMAC helpers
- scripts/terraform/validate_module_versions.sh (+80 lines) - script used in CI pipeline
- e2e/advisor/compatibility_test.go (+300 lines) - e2e scenarios for upgrade/downgrade checks
- docs/upgrade-safety-advisor.md (+900 lines) - user-facing documentation and remediation guide

Testing & CI:
- Added a new CI job 'installer-advisor-e2e' that runs the compatibility matrix in a lightweight KinD environment; job flaked on first run due to KinD image pull rate; retried and passed on run #2.
- Unit tests for diffing/parsing achieved ~92% coverage for new package.
- Integration tests validate that timed locks prevent concurrent restore attempts and auto-expire after configured TTL.

Discussion / Review thread (highlights):
- Evan Chu: "This is great — we need stronger signals for retrograde risk. A few asks: (1) can the advisor produce machine-parsable 'blockers' and 'advisories' so Console can auto-surface them? (2) add a --enforce flag so operators can opt into blocking behavior."
- Author (Priya): "Responded: added JSON schema for advisor reports and wired --enforce into the installer; default remains advisory-only to avoid surprising existing automation (see commit f2c19b2). Console wiring will be a follow-up (ENG-2204)."
- Leah Kim: "Please add an e2e that demonstrates a false-positive scenario and how operator remediation should proceed."
- Author: "Added a test scenario that includes a reversible migration and explicit remediation steps in docs. The test asserts advisor moves the report from 'blocking' to 'advisory' after a suggested migration is applied."
- Marcos Alvarez: "Security check: storing the lock in a ConfigMap is fine but we should HMAC and TTL it — ensure the HMAC key comes from the existing cluster secret (installer config) and rotateable via the installer's KMS integration."
- Author: "Implemented HMAC + TTL and added KMS rotation note in docs; rotation is out-of-scope for this PR but planned as follow-up."

CI status and notes:
- installer-lint: pass
- unit-tests: pass
- installer-advisor-e2e: pass (second try after rate limit backoff)
- helm-chart-ci: pass
- terraform-validate: pass (emits warnings for two third-party modules; warnings do not block merge)

Merge: Squash merge performed by maintainers after 2 approvals. Merge commit includes 'Enable advisor as opt-in; default advisory mode; timed restore locks enabled by default with configurable TTL.'

Follow-ups / TODOs:
- ENG-2204: Integrate advisor output into Redwood Console (auto-surface blockers and link remediation steps).
- ENG-2231: Add KMS-backed rotation flow and stronger secret lifecycle for HMAC keys used by restore locks.
- ENG-2240: Add an enforcement policy that can be centrally toggled for Dedicated customers in their control plane.

Notes for operators:
- Default behavior does not block upgrades; to enable blocking behavior set installer flag --advisor-enforce=true and configure enforcement policy in installer-config.yaml.
- Timed restore lock default TTL: 30 minutes; configurable via installer-config.restore_lock.ttl_seconds. If a restore is interrupted, operator can force-unlock after verifying cluster state using 'installer ops restore-unlock --force --reason <text>'.

Risk assessment: Low for default installs (advisory-only) but medium for users who opt into enforcement; operators should run the advisor as a preflight step in staging before enabling --advisor-enforce in production.

Changelog entry (user-facing): 'vNext: introduce Version Safety Advisor (preflight compatibility checks) and timed restore locks to reduce upgrade/restore races. Advisor is advisory-only by default; opt-in enforcement available.'

source.title: sealedsecret: safe-swap flow with kms fallback, probe job, and recovery playbooks
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.release_notes: Introduce a staged SealedSecret rotation flow with automatic fallback to KMS-wrapped values, pre-upgrade probes and documented recovery playbooks. No user-visible API changes.
source.description: Motivation: private deployments that use SealedSecrets + Vault/KMS face a race during TLS/KMS key rotations where consumers may receive partially-rotated secrets. This PR implements a safe 'swap' flow to allow staged SealedSecret rotation with an automated compatibility probe and a documented rollback/recovery path. Goals: 1) perform TLS/KMS-based rotation with zero-secret-latency windows for runtime; 2) detect keywrap/unwrap incompatibilities prior to cutover; 3) provide easy rollback and audit telemetry for operators. Changes are split across Helm templates, Helm hooks, a small controller-ish job for probing previously-rotated entries, Terraform variables for the installer, and runbooks/playbooks for operators. Checklist: - [x] Helm chart: new values for sealedsecret.swap.enabled, probeJob config, and pre-upgrade hook annotations - [x] Helm templates: introduce sealedsecret-safe-swap Job and associated RBAC - [x] Terraform: optional var enable_sealedsecret_safe_swap and minimal IAM/KMS policy docs - [x] Installer hooks: pre-upgrade probe step and post-failback cleanup - [x] Recovery playbooks: operator runbook for canary, rollback, and forced rewrap - [x] E2E smoke test added to ci/upgrade-tests that exercises probe job against a simulated KMS mismatch - [x] CI: new pipeline step added to run the probe job in the chart verification job Rationale/flow: 1) Deploy new chart with sealedsecret.swap.enabled=true; the chart installs a probe Job (disabled by default) and adds annotations to SealedSecrets managed by the installer to include a rotation metadata label. 2) Operator triggers rotation; the system creates rewrapped SealedSecret objects under a staging suffix and the probe Job attempts to unwrap them using the target KMS key material. 3) If all probe unwrappings succeed, a safe-swap controller (lightweight K8s Job run by Helm hook) performs an atomic replace of the live secrets; otherwise the probe returns detailed failure reasons and the installer can either rollback or continue to manual remediation. 4) If a failure occurs after partial cutover, recovery playbooks provide steps to: rewrap to prior key, promote canary nodes to traffic, and audit the event using the new telemetry labels. Telemetry: chart adds labels and emits events to the installer operator (via the probe Job logs) with structured messages that the Console can pick up (rotation.id=<uuid>, stage=probe|commit|rollback, failures=...). Security considerations: the probe Job has least-privilege IAM/KMS usage documented; secrets are never written to disk unencrypted and logs redact key material. Implementation notes: - The probe job is implemented as a small Go binary (probe-wrap-check) compiled into an image referenced in values.yaml. It performs KMS Unwrap in a dry-run mode and returns deterministic status codes. - Helm templates add a pre-upgrade hook that can be toggled off (useful for low-trust air-gapped upgrades). - Terraform variables add optional IAM role binding for probe job when enable_sealedsecret_safe_swap=true. Migration notes: existing installations must opt-in by setting values.sealedsecret.swap.enabled=true; default remains off to avoid surprising behavior. See docs/private-deployments/sealedsecret-safe-swap.md for operator guidance and playbooks.
commits: c4f2b9d: introduce probe-wrap-check binary and Dockerfile
7a1d2e3: add Helm values and templates for sealedsecret safe-swap job
9b3e8f0: terraform: add enable_sealedsecret_safe_swap var and IAM docs
2f5a0c1: installer: wire pre-upgrade hook and post-failback cleanup
e8d7c44: docs: operator runbook and run-through examples
b6a0f9e: ci: add upgrade-test that runs probe job in chart verification
review_comments: Arielle Gomez: Left a few nits on the probe job RBAC scope; prefer a dedicated KMS role instead of cluster-admin-flavored bindings.
Priya Natarajan: replied: updated role to kms-sealedsecret-probe-role and tightened serviceAccount binding per feedback.
Liam O'Connor: request_changes: add a failure-mode that clearly signals partial cutover and include a deterministic remediation command.
Priya Natarajan: pushed a follow-up commit that includes remediation cli 'installer sealedsecret rollback --rotation <id>' and explicit partial-cutover event reasons.
Hiroshi Tanaka: approval: Verified chart renders in CI and probe job exits 0 against mock KMS; LGTM after one small logging tweak.
CI Bot: build passed; integration tests for terraform module ran in ephemeral env and passed.
Docs reviewer (A. Mendes): small doc suggestion to include an example of rewrap TTL—addressed in latest doc commit.
source.merge_outcome: Merged via squash-merge by Priya Natarajan after approvals and green CI
source.notes_for_ops: Operators: to enable the safe-swap flow, set charts.private-installer.sealedsecret.swap.enabled=true and ensure the installer has a kms:Decrypt IAM role bound to the probe SA. Use the included playbook 'sealedsecret-safe-swap.md' before scheduling a rotation. If the probe reports 'unwrap-failure: key-version-mismatch', run 'installer sealedsecret rewrap --from <old-key> --to <new-key> --rotation <id>' to reconcile, then re-run the probe. Audit: rotation events include rotation.id label for correlation.
source.risk_and_mitigation: Risk: misconfigured KMS IAM could cause probe to fail and block automated cutover. Mitigation: probe is opt-in and chart will not alter live secrets until probe passes; there is a documented operator rollback path and automated cleanup hook.

Security considerations: the probe Job has least-privilege IAM/KMS usage documented; secrets are never written to disk unencrypted and logs redact key material. Implementation notes: - The probe job is implemented as a small Go binary (probe-wrap-check) compiled into an image referenced in values.yaml. It performs KMS Unwrap in a dry-run mode and returns deterministic status codes. - Helm templates add a pre-upgrade hook that can be toggled off (useful for low-trust air-gapped upgrades). - Terraform variables add optional IAM role binding for probe job when enable_sealedsecret_safe_swap=true. Migration notes: existing installations must opt-in by setting values.sealedsecret.swap.enabled=true; default remains off to avoid surprising behavior. See docs/private-deployments/sealedsecret-safe-swap.md for operator guidance and playbooks.

source.title: Add rollback dry-run emulator and Helm constraint enforcer
source.author: Priya N. Rao
source.description: Motivation: Upgrades for private deployments must have a low blast radius and the ability to verify a rollback without touching production state. This PR implements two complementary safety mechanisms: a rollback dry-run emulator (emulator) that simulates an in-place rollback sequence against an ephemeral clone of cluster state, and a Helm constraint enforcer that gates Helm value sets and chart versions during upgrade/rollback orchestration. Summary of changes: 1) installer/upgrade: add 'upgrade-dryrun-emulator' binary and operator API hooks. The emulator performs an automated sequence: snapshot export, ephemeral namespace restore, execute rollback steps (helm release rollback, CRD reduction/restore, DB migration reversal using migration runner in read-only mode), and post-run verification that checks service account bindings, KV cache compatibility, and storage layout. 2) operator: add UpgradePlan validation webhook that runs Helm constraint checks and consults chart version pinlist to block unsupported downgrades. 3) terraform: add plan-lock hook that fails concurrent apply attempts for clusters with an active UpgradePlan. 4) helm: add values schema and enforced constraints (persistence.enabled must be true for replicas > 1, image.tag pinning, pdb minimum available thresholds). 5) docs/tests: add detailed dry-run playbook and new e2e test which performs a synthetic rollback against a cloned Postgres dataset. Design notes: - The emulator is intentionally non-destructive: it uses snapshot/restore to a namespaced clone so no production DB, manifests, or PVs are modified. - Constraint enforcer exposes a 'strict' and 'permissive' mode; default is 'strict' for release channels tagged 'enterprise'. - Terraform plan-lock integrates with the existing state backend and writes an UpgradeLock object to the installer operator; this avoids split-brain upgrades. - All changes are configurable via operator Helm values under 'upgradeSafety'. How to test locally: - make dev-upgrade-emulator: runs integration-dryrun using testdata snapshots. - rictl upgrade dry-run --cluster <test-cluster> --plan <plan-id> will run the emulator and upload artifacts to the specified workspace. Backwards compatibility: the operator will reject UpgradePlans that request downgrades unless 'allowDowngrade' is explicitly set and an approved bypass token is provided. Rollout plan: merged to main, feature flagged off by default. Gradual enable for staging clusters, then opt-in for customers. Related: ENG-9321 (upgrade safety), ENG-9410 (helm gating), SUP-5482 (customer incident followup).
commits: init: add emulator CLI and operator hooks
feat(operator): upgradeplan webhook and constraint enforcer
feat(helm): values schema and example constraints
feat(terraform): plan-lock resource and backend hook
test: add e2e dry-run rollback test and fixtures
docs: dry-run playbook and troubleshooting guide
review_comments: Diego Morales: Can the emulator be run without a full DB snapshot? Author: yes; emulator supports logical pg_dump-based dry-run for smaller testcases
Ava Chen: Helm constraint enforcer should surface which specific constraint failed with a link to remediation steps. Action: added detailed error payloads and remediation links
Samir Patel: add metric for 'dryrun_success_rate' for monitoring. Action: added Prometheus metric and Grafana dashboard panel
QA: observed intermittent failure in e2e when underlying storage class reclaim policy is Delete; added additional cleanup and wait logic
source.ci_logs_summary: Most checks green. Integration dry-run initially flaked due to timing; fixed by adding wait for PV binding. Terraform plan step now emits a warning when plan-lock is present; CI runs in warn mode to allow merge after manual review.
source.merge_info: Merged by: Ava Chen; method: squash; commit: 'squash: add rollback dry-run emulator and helm constraint enforcer'
source.release_notes: Added a non-destructive rollback dry-run emulator and a Helm constraint enforcer to reduce upgrade/rollback risk for private deployments. New operator UpgradePlan validation and terraform plan-lock guard help prevent concurrent or unsupported upgrades. Includes CLI support (rictl upgrade dry-run) and e2e test coverage.
source.testing_and_validation: Unit tests added for webhook logic and emulator orchestration; integration-dryrun verifies end-to-end rollback simulation against an ephemeral Postgres clone; manual staging run performed on staging cluster 'stg-us-west-2' and results validated by SRE; promoted to canary after 48h with no regressions.
source.notes_for_operators: Feature disabled by default. To enable: set charts.private-installer.upgradeSafety.enabled=true and configure chartVersionPinlist and planLock.backend. If enabling in production, schedule a maintenance window and run rictl upgrade dry-run for each target cluster to collect artifacts before performing real upgrades.
source.changelog_entry: Implement rollback dry-run emulator and Helm constraint enforcement to reduce upgrade blast radius; add terraform plan-lock integration; add rictl dry-run command and docs.
source.risk_assessment: Low-medium: emulator performs non-destructive operations; however, added operator webhooks and terraform hooks change upgrade gating behavior and must be carefully configured. Default keeps safety checks off for self-hosted until explicitly turned on.
post_merge_tasks: create linear task to add UI support in Console for dry-run artifacts (ENG-9570)

source.title: hsm-keystore-sync-operator-add-rotation-plan-and-sealedsecret-migration
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.description: Adds an idempotent HSM Keystore Sync Operator plus Helm and Terraform changes to automate certificate and keystore rotation for private deployments. Motivation: manual keystore rotation across on-prem HSMs and cloud KMS boundaries has been a high-friction operational task that caused outages during upgrades and prevented safe automated rollouts. This PR implements: 1) a lightweight operator (hsm-keystore-sync) that watches configured HSM instances and Vault/Transit endpoints, reconciles keystore/JKS/PKCS12 artifacts into a namespace-safe secret, and annotates resources with rotation metadata; 2) Helm pre-upgrade/post-upgrade hooks that perform staged keystore swaps and validate TLS handshake before switching live listeners; 3) a sealedsecret-to-vault-migration tool that converts selected SealedSecrets into Vault-backed secrets using an adoption plan and write-lock to avoid races; 4) Terraform module outputs and state migration helpers to surface keystore_version and last_rotated timestamps so Dedicated customers can integrate with external rotation policies; 5) comprehensive integration tests and a chaos test that simulates HSM failover and verifies graceful fallback to a cached KEK. This change is designed to be rollback-safe: keystore swaps are implemented as two-phase commits with ability to revert to previous secret if validation fails. Upgrade notes and operator config examples are in the README and chart values. The description includes a checklist, test plan, and rollout/rollback steps.
source.release_notes: Private-installer: add hsm-keystore-sync operator to automate HSM/JKS/PKCS12 rotation; Helm hooks for safe pre-upgrade keystore swaps; sealedsecret->vault migration helper; terraform outputs to expose keystore_version and last_rotated. No breaking changes; opt-in via chart values.
commits: feat(operator): initial hsm-keystore-sync controller and CRD with reconcile loop
chore(helm): add pre-upgrade/post-upgrade hook jobs and validation containers
feat(migration): sealedsecret-to-vault migration helper and adoption locking
feat(terraform): expose keystore_version/last_rotated outputs and state import helper
test(integration): add daily integration test exercising HSM failover path
docs: examples and upgrade notes for on-prem and multi-az deployments
review_comments: Marco Alvarez: Operator looks good but we need a clear audit trail for each rotation event. Please add an event and metric (keystore.rotation.count) - addressed in second commit.

Liu Chen: How do we ensure the operator does not leak private key material into logs or metrics? Author: encrypted-bytes are never logged and metrics are counts only; added e2e that verifies none of the pods contain PEM in plain-text - see tests/integration

Aisha Mbaye: Terraform output change may require state import docs for on-prem customers. Author: added terraform/modules/kms-keystore/README.md with import steps and an automated import helper script.

Marco Alvarez: Please add a canary rollout plan to the chart values so enterprise customers can opt into staged rotation. Author: added values.keystore.canaryPercentage and documented steps in README-keystore-operator.md.
source.notes_for_ops: Operator is opt-in via chart value values.keystore.enabled=true. Default behavior is no-op until a Keystore CR is created. Rollout plan: 1) enable operator in staging; 2) create Keystore CR with dryRun=true to validate swap; 3) run pre-upgrade hook job to stage keystore; 4) flip live listener after TLS validation passes; 5) monitor metric keystore.rotation.count and events for audit. Rollback: chart values.keystore.rollbackVersion set to previous version triggers two-phase revert. For Terraform, run terraform import module.kms_keystore.<name> before applying new outputs. For sealedsecret migration, use tools/sealedsecret-migrate with --dry-run then --apply once validated.
source.notes_for_devs: Design notes: operator uses the HSM plugin interface and Vault Transit to fetch wrapped keys; it stores only encrypted keystore blobs in Kubernetes secrets and annotates with keystore.v1.redwood.com/last-rotated. The controller includes leader election and a reconciliation window to avoid thundering herd. Tests added for idempotency and HSM connection drops.
source.changelog_excerpt: Added hsm-keystore-sync operator; helm hooks for safe keystore rotation; sealedsecret->vault migration helper; terraform outputs for keystore visibility.
source.testing_summary: Unit tests added for controller logic and migration tool; integration e2e simulates HSM primary failover and verifies TLS handshakes succeed with swapped keystore; CI runs include a simulated low-latency network partition test.
source.support_notes: If customers report TLS handshake failures after upgrade, verify operator logs (level=info) and events on the Keystore CR, then run the validation job included in the chart. For HSM keywrap mismatches, ensure KEK version in Vault matches the HSM-attested KEK and run the migration helper.
source.user_migration_instructions: 1) Set values.keystore.enabled=true in your private-installer chart and set values.keystore.canaryPercentage=10 for initial rollout; 2) Create a Keystore CR with reference to your HSM and transit path; 3) Run helm upgrade with --wait to execute the pre-upgrade hook; 4) Validate TLS endpoints with the provided validation container; 5) For sealedsecrets migration, run tools/sealedsecret-migrate --namespace <ns> --selector app=my-app --dry-run then --apply once review passes.
follow_up_tasks: ENG-2371: Expose keystore rotation metrics to Redwood Console per-route
ENG-2422: Add IAM connector for AWS CloudHSM fallback
ENG-2450: Operator TLS cert auto-renew support with ACME for non-HSM cases

source.title: Add tenant snapshot coordinator and staged rollback gatekeeper
source.author: Priya Nair
source.release_notes: Introduce a tenant-scoped snapshot coordinator and staged rollback gating mechanism. Adds helm hooks and a TF module pin to enable safe preview restores and operator quiesce during upgrades. Backwards-compatible by default; no config changes required for existing installs.
source.description:
Motivation: Private upgrades for multi-tenant on-prem deployments need lower blast radius and safer downgrade paths. This PR introduces a tenant-scoped SnapshotCoordinator CRD/controller and a staged rollback Gatekeeper that validates restore readiness and prevents accidental downgrades. The goal is to enable preflight restore rehearsals and automatic gating during multi-tenant upgrades so operators can abort or stage rollbacks without impacting healthy tenants.

What this changes (high level):
- New CRD: TenantSnapshotRequest (api/v1alpha1) and controller that serializes snapshot creation per tenant, signs artifacts with release metadata, and records tenant-impact score.
- New controller: SnapshotCoordinator that can schedule rehearsal restores into an isolated test namespace (operator-managed) and produce a RestoreReadinessReport.
- Rollback gatekeeper: Operator-side admission webhook + controller that consults latest RestoreReadinessReport and a version-compatibility matrix before allowing a rollback/downgrade to proceed.
- Helm: add new chart hooks (pre-upgrade/post-upgrade) to trigger snapshot requests and to wait for gatekeeper release-checks; hooks time out configurable via values.yaml (default 30m).
- Terraform: pin module redwood/postgres-cluster to 2.4.1 and add plan-time validation step to warn on incompatible operator/version combos.
- Installer: add operator-quiesce job and optional tenant-quarantine flag to prevent write traffic during rehearsals.

Commits (summary):
- 9c3b2f7 Add TenantSnapshotRequest CRD and types
- 8f1a4d0 Implement SnapshotCoordinator controller and exporter for RestoreReadinessReport
- 7b2dffd Add Gatekeeper admission webhook and downgrade-checking logic
- d4a1e33 Helm: hooks + values for staged rollback flow
- a92f0c1 Terraform: pin module and add validation script
- 11b3a6f Add e2e rehearsal test harness and CI job definition

Files changed (high-level):
- charts/private/values.yaml (new hooks, timeouts)
- charts/private/templates/hooks.yaml (pre/post hooks and job templates)
- operator/crds/tenant_snapshotrequest.yaml (CRD)
- operator/controllers/snapshotcoordinator/* (controller, reconciler, exporter)
- operator/controllers/gatekeeper/* (webhook, checks)
- infra/terraform/modules/postgres/versions.tf (module pin)
- test/e2e/rehearsal_restore_test.go (new e2e)

Tests and CI:
- Added an e2e rehearsal restore test that runs against KIND; marked as slow and gated behind nightly.
- CI initially failed due to a flaky admission webhook test (race on webhook cache invalidation). I added a retry in the test harness and a short backoff in the controller startup sequence. Re-ran CI: all checks passed.

Migration and backward compatibility:
- CRD is v1alpha1; controller gracefully ignores TenantSnapshotRequests if operator lacks feature-flag. No automatic changes to existing resources.
- Helm defaults preserve current behavior; operators opt into staged rollback by setting values.stagedRollback.enabled=true.
- Terraform pin prevents inadvertent upgrades of the postgres module without an explicit bump in the installer.

Security considerations:
- Snapshot artifacts are signed with cluster release metadata and stored in tenant-scoped object paths. Signing uses existing KMS integration; no new secrets introduced.
- Admission webhook uses existing RBAC roles. Webhook is optional (values.webhook.enabled) for air-gapped installs where dynamic admission is disabled.

Review thread (highlights):
- Ava Chen (reviewer): "Can the coordinator handle concurrent snapshot requests across 200 tenants without saturating the PG replica?"
  Priya: "Coordinator uses a leaky-bucket rate limiter and a locality-aware queue; added config values.queue.concurrencyPerRegion with sensible defaults. Added performance note in README and a test simulating 250 concurrent requests."
- Marcus Li: "What happens if a rehearsal restore times out? Will we leave test namespaces around?"
  Priya: "Controller schedules a TTL garbage-collect job for rehearsal namespaces and marks RestoreReadinessReport.status=TimedOut; the helm hook will fail the upgrade if required thresholds aren't met. Added a finalizer to ensure cleanup on controller crash."
- Diego Morales: "Terraform pin looks fine but add a plan-time check so operators aren't surprised."
  Priya: "Added infra/terraform/scripts/validate_compat.sh and integrated it into the installer plan step; it returns non-zero on obvious mismatches but is non-fatal by default (configurable)."

Follow-ups (todo items captured in PR):
- ENG-2576: Add a compact dashboard in Console for RestoreReadinessReport per tenant (linked in linear).
- ENG-2634: Introduce opt-in dry-run for helm hooks so large clusters can stage without affecting live traffic.
- Add benchmarks for snapshot throughput vs. postgres replica lag; suggested by Ava.

Why this is different from previous work: Many previous PRs focused on transactional DB migration helpers and snapshot signing. This PR stitches several pieces into an operator-driven rehearsal workflow with an admission-time rollback gate — the focus is on staged, tenant-scoped rehearsals and an admission policy that prevents unsafe downgrades across tenants. The helm+terraform+operator changes are coordinated to provide a minimal-ops path for operators to opt into safe rollbacks.

Rollback/upgrade playbook (short):
1) Set values.stagedRollback.enabled=true in the helm release values and apply helm upgrade --wait.
2) Helm pre-upgrade hook creates TenantSnapshotRequests for high-risk tenants (based on tenant impact score).
3) SnapshotCoordinator runs rehearsal restores into isolated test namespaces and emits RestoreReadinessReports.
4) Gatekeeper validates readiness and compatibility; if checks pass, the upgrade proceeds. If not, gatekeeper blocks rollback/downgrade and prints guidance to the operator.

Merge notes: Squash-merged into main after approvals from Ava and Marcus. CI green on merge. Cherry-pick guidance included for release branches in the PR comments.

If you want to try locally: follow operator/README.md sections "rehearsal mode" and "helm staged rollback". The e2e requires KIND and about 12GB RAM to run the rehearsal harness.

- ENG-2576: Add a compact dashboard in Console for RestoreReadinessReport per tenant (linked in linear).
- ENG-2634: Introduce opt-in dry-run for helm hooks so large clusters can stage without affecting live traffic.
- Add benchmarks for snapshot throughput vs. postgres replica lag; suggested by Ava.

Why this is different from previous work: Many previous PRs focused on transactional DB migration helpers and snapshot signing. This PR stitches several pieces into an operator-driven rehearsal workflow with an admission-time rollback gate — the focus is on staged, tenant-scoped rehearsals and an admission policy that prevents unsafe downgrades across tenants. The helm+terraform+operator changes are coordinated to provide a minimal-ops path for operators to opt into safe rollbacks.

Rollback/upgrade playbook (short):
1) Set values.stagedRollback.enabled=true in the helm release values and apply helm upgrade --wait.
2) Helm pre-upgrade hook creates TenantSnapshotRequests for high-risk tenants (based on tenant impact score).
3) SnapshotCoordinator runs rehearsal restores into isolated test namespaces and emits RestoreReadinessReports.
4) Gatekeeper validates readiness and compatibility; if checks pass, the upgrade proceeds. If not, gatekeeper blocks rollback/downgrade and prints guidance to the operator.

Merge notes: Squash-merged into main after approvals from Ava and Marcus. CI green on merge. Cherry-pick guidance included for release branches in the PR comments.

If you want to try locally: follow operator/README.md sections "rehearsal mode" and "helm staged rollback". The e2e requires KIND and about 12GB RAM to run the rehearsal harness.
text:
Motivation: Private upgrades for multi-tenant on-prem deployments need lower blast radius and safer downgrade paths. This PR introduces a tenant-scoped SnapshotCoordinator CRD/controller and a staged rollback Gatekeeper that validates restore readiness and prevents accidental downgrades. The goal is to enable preflight restore rehearsals and automatic gating during multi-tenant upgrades so operators can abort or stage rollbacks without impacting healthy tenants.

What this changes (high level):
- New CRD: TenantSnapshotRequest (api/v1alpha1) and controller that serializes snapshot creation per tenant, signs artifacts with release metadata, and records tenant-impact score.
- New controller: SnapshotCoordinator that can schedule rehearsal restores into an isolated test namespace (operator-managed) and produce a RestoreReadinessReport.
- Rollback gatekeeper: Operator-side admission webhook + controller that consults latest RestoreReadinessReport and a version-compatibility matrix before allowing a rollback/downgrade to proceed.
- Helm: add new chart hooks (pre-upgrade/post-upgrade) to trigger snapshot requests and to wait for gatekeeper release-checks; hooks time out configurable via values.yaml (default 30m).
- Terraform: pin module redwood/postgres-cluster to 2.4.1 and add plan-time validation step to warn on incompatible operator/version combos.
- Installer: add operator-quiesce job and optional tenant-quarantine flag to prevent write traffic during rehearsals.

Commits (summary):
- 9c3b2f7 Add TenantSnapshotRequest CRD and types
- 8f1a4d0 Implement SnapshotCoordinator controller and exporter for RestoreReadinessReport
- 7b2dffd Add Gatekeeper admission webhook and downgrade-checking logic
- d4a1e33 Helm: hooks + values for staged rollback flow
- a92f0c1 Terraform: pin module and add validation script
- 11b3a6f Add e2e rehearsal test harness and CI job definition

Files changed (high-level):
- charts/private/values.yaml (new hooks, timeouts)
- charts/private/templates/hooks.yaml (pre/post hooks and job templates)
- operator/crds/tenant_snapshotrequest.yaml (CRD)
- operator/controllers/snapshotcoordinator/* (controller, reconciler, exporter)
- operator/controllers/gatekeeper/* (webhook, checks)
- infra/terraform/modules/postgres/versions.tf (module pin)
- test/e2e/rehearsal_restore_test.go (new e2e)

Tests and CI:
- Added an e2e rehearsal restore test that runs against KIND; marked as slow and gated behind nightly.
- CI initially failed due to a flaky admission webhook test (race on webhook cache invalidation). I added a retry in the test harness and a short backoff in the controller startup sequence. Re-ran CI: all checks passed.

Migration and backward compatibility:
- CRD is v1alpha1; controller gracefully ignores TenantSnapshotRequests if operator lacks feature-flag. No automatic changes to existing resources.
- Helm defaults preserve current behavior; operators opt into staged rollback by setting values.stagedRollback.enabled=true.
- Terraform pin prevents inadvertent upgrades of the postgres module without an explicit bump in the installer.

Security considerations:
- Snapshot artifacts are signed with cluster release metadata and stored in tenant-scoped object paths. Signing uses existing KMS integration; no new secrets introduced.
- Admission webhook uses existing RBAC roles. Webhook is optional (values.webhook.enabled) for air-gapped installs where dynamic admission is disabled.

Review thread (highlights):
- Ava Chen (reviewer): "Can the coordinator handle concurrent snapshot requests across 200 tenants without saturating the PG replica?"
  Priya: "Coordinator uses a leaky-bucket rate limiter and a locality-aware queue; added config values.queue.concurrencyPerRegion with sensible defaults. Added performance note in README and a test simulating 250 concurrent requests."
- Marcus Li: "What happens if a rehearsal restore times out? Will we leave test namespaces around?"
  Priya: "Controller schedules a TTL garbage-collect job for rehearsal namespaces and marks RestoreReadinessReport.status=TimedOut; the helm hook will fail the upgrade if required thresholds aren't met. Added a finalizer to ensure cleanup on controller crash."
- Diego Morales: "Terraform pin looks fine but add a plan-time check so operators aren't surprised."
  Priya: "Added infra/terraform/scripts/validate_compat.sh and integrated it into the installer plan step; it returns non-zero on obvious mismatches but is non-fatal by default (configurable)."

Follow-ups (todo items captured in PR):
- ENG-2576: Add a compact dashboard in Console for RestoreReadinessReport per tenant (linked in linear).
- ENG-2634: Introduce opt-in dry-run for helm hooks so large clusters can stage without affecting live traffic.
- Add benchmarks for snapshot throughput vs. postgres replica lag; suggested by Ava.

Why this is different from previous work: Many previous PRs focused on transactional DB migration helpers and snapshot signing. This PR stitches several pieces into an operator-driven rehearsal workflow with an admission-time rollback gate — the focus is on staged, tenant-scoped rehearsals and an admission policy that prevents unsafe downgrades across tenants. The helm+terraform+operator changes are coordinated to provide a minimal-ops path for operators to opt into safe rollbacks.

Rollback/upgrade playbook (short):
1) Set values.stagedRollback.enabled=true in the helm release values and apply helm upgrade --wait.
2) Helm pre-upgrade hook creates TenantSnapshotRequests for high-risk tenants (based on tenant impact score).
3) SnapshotCoordinator runs rehearsal restores into isolated test namespaces and emits RestoreReadinessReports.
4) Gatekeeper validates readiness and compatibility; if checks pass, the upgrade proceeds. If not, gatekeeper blocks rollback/downgrade and prints guidance to the operator.

Merge notes: Squash-merged into main after approvals from Ava and Marcus. CI green on merge. Cherry-pick guidance included for release branches in the PR comments.

If you want to try locally: follow operator/README.md sections "rehearsal mode" and "helm staged rollback". The e2e requires KIND and about 12GB RAM to run the rehearsal harness.

source.title: add-schema-compatibility-checker-and-restore-validation
source.author: Jordan Wells
source.release_notes: Adds a pre-upgrade schema compatibility validator, automated pre-upgrade backup hooks, and post-restore verification to reduce upgrade/downgrade risk.
source.description:
Motivation: We observed multiple upgrade incidents where subtle schema changes (nullable -> not-null, removed columns, implicit type narrowing) caused partial upgrades and difficult rollbacks. This PR introduces an automated pre-upgrade compatibility check and a post-restore validation step to make private installer upgrades safer and more auditable.

What this PR does: Adds a schema compatibility checker that runs as part of the Helm pre-upgrade hook and the Terraform upgrade plan job. The checker compares the target release's migration manifest with a database introspection snapshot to detect incompatible operations (destructive drops, narrowing type changes, non-null constraints without defaults). If an incompatibility is detected, the hook fails the upgrade and emits diagnostic artifacts (schema-diff.json, sample rows) into the release-namespace PVC.

Backup/restore automation: Introduces a pre-upgrade backup job (helm hook) that triggers an atomic snapshot export of Postgres WAL and a compressed dump for on-prem environments; the job uploads artifacts to the configured artifacts bucket or local NFS. A new post-restore verification job validates critical invariants (row counts for "tenants" and "deployments" tables, checksum of configuration blob, presence of required indexes). The verification job returns non-zero on mismatch and writes a short report to the same artifacts location.

Schema compatibility rules: Basic rules implemented: (1) dropping columns is flagged as unsafe; (2) removing NOT NULL without default is flagged as a breaking change; (3) narrowing integer types or reducing varchar length is flagged; (4) adding columns with default/nullable is allowed. The rules are configurable via values.yaml: upgradeSanity.rules.allowUnsafe and upgradeSanity.rules.customWhitelist. Teams can opt-in to allow-unsafe for emergency upgrades using a documented flag and an explicit override token.

Installer changes: Helm charts: added pre-upgrade and post-restore hook jobs, new templates for schema-diff uploads, and values knobs. Terraform: added state-lock verification before terraform apply and an optional module input to enable remote snapshot validation in IaC pipelines. Installer CLI: new subcommand redwood-installer verify-upgrade --target-version that runs the same checks locally against kubeconfig for offline validation.

Observability: Emits Prometheus metrics upgrade_sanity_check_total, upgrade_sanity_check_failures, backup_hook_duration_seconds, restore_validation_failures; logs include a structured reason code (SCHEMA_DROP, TYPE_NARROW, NOT_NULL_ADD) to assist incident triage.

Testing: Unit tests for compatibility rules and integration tests covering helm pre-upgrade hook execution on KinD: we added a CI matrix job e2e/restore-canary which runs a simulated backup+restore against a seeded dataset. The first CI run showed a flaky timeout in restore verification which was fixed by raising the default restore timeout and adding retries for eventual index creation.

Operational notes: The default behavior blocks upgrades that are potentially unsafe. For existing large customers, we ship a migration report generator (redwood-installer migration-report) that operators can run to get a human-readable plan and the list of incompatible actions.

Docs and rollout: Added docs/private-upgrades.md and upgraded the upgrade checklist in docs/operational-playbooks/upgrade-checklist.md to include running verify-upgrade and saving the backup artifact manifest.

Rollback plan: On failure, the pre-upgrade hook prevents the release from being applied. If an operator performed an emergency override and later needs to rollback, the post-upgrade artifacts include an automatic restore script and a validation report to verify the rollback restored invariants.

CI/QA: See tests_run for details. This change does not modify runtime code paths for serving; it is focused on installer and orchestration.
commits: a1b2c3d: add schema compatibility checker binary and CLI integration
d4e5f6a: helm: add pre-upgrade backup hook and schema-diff uploader templates
b7c8d9e: terraform: add state-lock verification and optional snapshot validation input
f0a1b2c: installer-cli: add verify-upgrade subcommand and migration-report generator
c3d4e5f: tests: add unit tests for compatibility rules and e2e/restore-canary CI job
e6f7a8b: docs: private-upgrades.md and upgrade-checklist updates
diff_snippets:
+++ helm/redwood/templates/hooks/pre-upgrade-backup-job.yaml
+ job: |
+   apiVersion: batch/v1
+   kind: Job
+   metadata:
+     name: pre-upgrade-backup-{{ .Release.Name }}
review_comments: Ava Chen (2026-02-14): This reads well, but can we make the ruleset extensible so platform teams can add custom validators?
Jordan Wells (2026-02-14): Yes — added upgradeSanity.rules.customWhitelist and plugin hook in compat_checker.go (see commits).
Miguel Torres (2026-02-17): CI e2e/restore-canary failed on first run with restore timeout; please add retry and increase default timeout for on-prem restores.
Jordan Wells (2026-02-18): Increased timeout and added 3 retries for restore verification. Re-ran CI, green.
Priya Nair (2026-02-21): Approving after test coverage numbers look reasonable and docs updated.
source.merge_outcome: Merged via squash. CI green after restore timeout fix. Merge commit recorded in main.
source.rollback_plan: If pre-upgrade hook fails, operator fixes incompatibilities or uses an explicit override token documented in private-upgrades.md. For emergency rollbacks after an override, run redwood-installer restore --artifact <backup-manifest> and verify with redwood-installer verify-restore. Support will triage SUP-1246 if artifacts are missing.
source.tests_summary: Unit coverage + compatibility rule permutations added. e2e restore canary validates backup/restore end-to-end against seeded dataset. First CI run failed due to timeout fixed by retry/backoff; subsequent runs green.
notes: Operators should set values.upgradeSanity.backupRetentionDays appropriately for on-prem storage cost constraints
We intentionally default to blocking unsafe upgrades to force explicit operator acknowledgement for risky changes

source.title: controlplane-subnet-failover-sentinel-and-route-priority-operator
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.release_notes: Adds a control-plane subnet failover sentinel, conditional route-priority operator, Terraform lease resource, and Helm pre-drain hooks to enable automated AZ failover and safe DNS cutover for private deployments.
source.description: Context: Customers running Redwood Private across multi-AZ VPCs require deterministic and low-risk control-plane failover when an AZ becomes degraded. This PR introduces a failover sentinel and a route-priority operator that coordinate subnet handoff, ENI lease transfer, conditional peering/route adjustments, and a preflight DNS cutover to minimize downtime and avoid split-brain. Motivation: Previous incidents showed manual subnet reallocation and DNS changes were error-prone and caused long recovery windows. We need an automated but controlled mechanism that integrates with the installer, Terraform modules, and Helm hooks so we can perform staged failovers with canary verification and easy rollback. Summary of changes: 1) New sentinel service (installer/cmd/sentinel) that monitors per-AZ ENI health, control-plane pod readiness, and VPC route table metrics; it evaluates failover conditions and triggers a coordinated plan. 2) Route-priority operator (installer/pkg/network/route_operator) that programs route table priorities and temporary peering routes to move control-plane egress to a standby AZ without tearing down existing endpoints. 3) Terraform module: vpc/subnet-lease.tf introduces a Lease resource for ENI/control-plane IPowner claims, with idempotent apply/rollback semantics and preflight plan output. 4) Helm chart: charts/controlplane/templates/pre-drain-hook.yaml adds a pre-drain hook that quiesces control-plane components and emits a checkpoint event before subnet swap. 5) DNS safety: preflight Route53 change batch dry-run and staged record TTL reduction automation to avoid cache poisoning during cutover. 6) Installer integration: new CLI flags and a dry-run mode for staged cutovers, plus detailed operator annotations for manual override. Rollout plan: start with canary in staging (1 cluster), then opt-in beta for customers with a flag, finally enabled by default after 2 weeks of telemetry. Includes a manual abort and automatic rollback if health checks fail. Testing and CI: unit tests for sentinel heuristics, Terraform plan/unit tests, Helm template tests, and an e2e canary that simulates AZ failure; CI pipeline required two runs (first run e2e failed due to timing assumptions, second run passed after adjusting backoff). Backwards compatibility: designed to be opt-in; existing installs not using the Lease resource remain unchanged until Terraform is applied. Operational docs: adds a runbook for forced cutover and a postmortem checklist to verify DNS propagation and route table state. Security: lease operations are audited, and KMS-encrypted state is used for critical actions.

source.title: manifest-diff-builder and KMS-stamped attestations for incremental private-installer releases
source.author: Ava Chen
source.description: Motivation: make private-installer releases incremental and cryptographically auditable without re-bundling full artifacts for every patch. This PR implements a manifest-diff builder along with KMS-stamped attestations for every delta to enable fast rollforward/rollback and compact provenance storage. Summary: add a new release tool 'manifest-diff' that computes a deterministic delta index between two installer builds, produce a compact delta tarball and an accompanying attest.json signed via customer-configured KMS (supports AWS KMS and Vault HSM). The pipeline integrates the diff artifacts into the release graph and updates release-manifests.yaml with delta entries and signature metadata. Commits summary: 1) initial manifest-diff implementation and tests; 2) KMS attestation client and integration layer; 3) CI pipeline changes to upload delta artifacts to release-bucket and verify attestations in staged job; 4) docs and upgrade notes; 5) small fixes for idempotent replay and key rotation handling. Files changed (high level): tools/manifest-diff/main.go added; tools/manifest-diff/README.md added; internal/release/delta_index.go added; internal/release/attest_kms.go added; ci/pipelines/release-delta.yml modified; charts/installer/values.yaml bumped for delta-recovery hooks; docs/private-installer/release-delta.md added; tests/fixtures/deltas/* added; scripts/verify-attest.sh added. Small illustrative snippet: delta_index entries are structured as {"from":"sha256:...","to":"sha256:...","files":[{"path":"charts/installer.tgz","type":"tar","digest":"sha256:...","size":12345}],"attests":[{"kind":"kms","uri":"awskms://arn:aws:kms:...","sig":"base64..."}]} Highlights and decisions: 1) Use KMS-stamped attestations rather than embedding private keys in CI; CI signs the attestation blob via customer-supplied KMS credential or a staging HSM; 2) Deltas are additive by file path and deterministic by sorted manifest entries to ensure stable signatures; 3) Release manifest schema extended with 'delta_index' block and backward-compatible fields; 4) Added verification step to release pipeline to fail if attestation verification fails. Testing: unit tests for delta computation and attestation verification, integration run against a synthetic release pair, at-scale smoke run on ci-stage with 3 sample deltas. Backwards compat: release consumers that do not understand delta entries will continue to use full artifacts; new clients can fetch and apply deltas. Rollout plan: merge to main, enable opt-in delta builds behind a feature flag in release pipeline for two weeks, run mirror promotion to ensure consumers can fall back to full artifacts. Follow-ups: add in-toto statement mapping and OCI registry push for delta tarballs (ENG-3378), add Vault auto-rotation support. Review thread summary: Bethany Li requested explicit handling for KMS key rotation and a replay protection counter in attestations; author implemented 'key_meta' and 'replay_nonce' fields and added tests. Rishi asked for stronger CI gating; pipeline now has attestation-verify job that runs before artifact-promotion and is green on ci-stage. Diego noted performance concerns for large releases; manifest-diff now streams file lists and avoids loading full file contents into memory. Approvals: Rishi Patel approved after CI green and dex fixes; Bethany Li approved after key rotation tests; Diego Morales approved after small performance tweak. Merge: squashed and merged to main via squash-merge. Release notes: "Adds manifest-diff builder and KMS-stamped attestations enabling incremental private-installer releases and compact provenance. Delta artifacts are optional and gated behind release flag." Associated follow-ups: ENG-3378 for OCI delta registry, ENG-3421 to add Vault auto-rotation. CI Summary: initial lint failed on goimports, fixed in commit 3; one flaky integration test retried and passed; final ci-status is pass. Merge metadata: method=squash, sha=abcd1234efgh, merged_by=Rishi Patel. Additional operational notes: operators should add allowed KMS principals to release pipeline role and rotate keys using the documented key_meta guidelines; a migration script to backfill delta_index for recent 5 releases is included in scripts/backfill-deltas.sh.

title: manifest-diff-builder and KMS-stamped attestations for incremental private-installer releases
preview: Motivation: make private-installer releases incremental and cryptographically auditable without re-bundling full artifacts for every patch. This PR implements a manifest-diff builder along with KMS-stamped attestations for every delta to enable fast rollforward/rollback and compact provenance storage. Summary: add a new release tool 'manifest-diff' that computes a deterministic delta index between two installer builds, produce a compact delta tarball and an accompanying attest.json signed via customer-configured KMS (supports AWS KMS and Vault HSM). The pipeline integrates the diff artifacts into the release graph and updates release-manifests.yaml with delta entries and signature metadata. Commits summary: 1) initial manifest-diff implementation and tests; 2) KMS attestation client and integration layer; 3) CI pipeline changes to upload delta artifacts to release-bucket and verify attestations in staged job; 4) docs and upgrade notes; 5) small fixes for idempotent replay and key rotation handling. Files changed (high level): tools/manifest-diff/main.go added; tools/manifest-diff/README.md added; internal/release/delta_index.go added; internal/release/attest_kms.go added; ci/pipelines/release-delta.yml modified; charts/installer/values.yaml bumped for delta-recovery hooks; docs/private-installer/release-delta.md added; tests/fixtures/deltas/* added; scripts/verify-attest.sh added. Small illustrative snippet: delta_index entries are structured as {"from":"sha256:...","to":"sha256:...","files":[{"path":"charts/installer.tgz","type":"tar","digest":"sha256:...","size":12345}],"attests":[{"kind":"kms","uri":"awskms://arn:aws:kms:...","sig":"base64..."}]} Highlights and decisions: 1) Use KMS-stamped attestations rather than embedding private keys in CI; CI signs the attestation blob via customer-supplied KMS credential or a staging HSM; 2) Deltas are additive by file path and deterministic by sorted manifest entries to ensure stable signatures; 3) Release manifest schema extended with 'delta_index' block and backward-compatible fields; 4) Added verification step to release pipeline to fail if attestation verification fails. Testing: unit tests for delta computation and attestation verification, integration run against a synthetic release pair, at-scale smoke run on ci-stage with 3 sample deltas. Backwards compat: release consumers that do not understand delta entries will continue to use full artifacts; new clients can fetch and apply deltas. Rollout plan: merge to main, enable opt-in delta builds behind a feature flag in release pipeline for two weeks, run mirror promotion to ensure consumers can fall back to full artifacts. Follow-ups: add in-toto statement mapping and OCI registry push for delta tarballs (ENG-3378), add Vault auto-rotation support. Review thread summary: Bethany Li requested explicit handling for KMS key rotation and a replay protection counter in attestations; author implemented 'key_meta' and 'replay_nonce' fields and added tests. Rishi asked for stronger CI gating; pipeline now has attestation-verify job that runs before artifact-promotion and is green on ci-stage. Diego noted performance concerns for large releases; manifest-diff now streams file lists and avoids loading full file contents into memory. Approvals: Rishi Patel approved after CI green and dex fixes; Bethany Li approved after key rotation tests; Diego Morales approved after small performance tweak. Merge: squashed and merged to main via squash-merge. Release notes: "Adds manifest-diff builder and KMS-stamped attestations enabling incremental private-installer releases and compact provenance. Delta artifacts are optional and gated behind release flag." Associated follow-ups: ENG-3378 for OCI delta registry, ENG-3421 to add Vault auto-rotation. CI Summary: initial lint failed on goimports, fixed in commit 3; one flaky integration test retried and passed; final ci-status is pass. Merge metadata: method=squash, sha=abcd1234efgh, merged_by=Rishi Patel. Additional operational notes: operators should add allowed KMS principals to release pipeline role and rotate keys using the documented key_meta guidelines; a migration script to backfill delta_index for recent 5 releases is included in scripts/backfill-deltas.sh.

source.title: microk8s air-gapped runtime bundle, delta patcher, and Terraform provider fixes
source.author: Lina Ortega
source.release_notes: Adds official microk8s air-gapped bundle and Terraform/provider compatibility; reduces offline bundle size via delta patching; installer now performs microk8s-specific preflight checks.
source.description: Motivation: customers running on-prem developer clusters requested an officially supported microk8s air-gapped installer path. This PR introduces a microk8s-targeted offline bundle, a delta-based patcher to dramatically reduce archive size for incremental updates, Terraform/provider fixes to surface registry mirror and snap-specific hooks, and Helm chart adjustments so the runtime works consistently with microk8s networking and CRI defaults. Work includes: 1) new bundle layout under installer/airgap/bundles/microk8s with slim OCI layers and explicit snap hooks, 2) a delta_patcher service that computes binary deltas between bundle versions and an unpack-time apply step to keep download footprint small, 3) Terraform module changes to support microk8s node labeling, registry mirror variables, and a provider shim for the installer to write local registries, 4) Helm chart compatibility bits (service type normalization, tolerations, and adjusted readiness probes), 5) installer preflight checks that detect microk8s channel, snap versions, and available disk space, 6) test harness updates and CI job matrix expansion to include microk8s (snap) smoke tests. Checklist: - [x] bundle creation and signing - [x] delta patcher implementation and unit tests - [x] Terraform module updates and docs - [x] e2e smoke tests on microk8s-ubuntu and microk8s-coreos - [x] QA signoff and staged rollout plan.
source.commits: add: initial microk8s bundle layout and manifest generation | feat(patcher): delta_patcher implementation + unit tests | fix(terraform): registry mirror variables and provider shim | chore(ci): microk8s smoke job in helm-integration workflow | docs: microk8s install docs and troubleshooting notes
review_comments: Ava Chen: Can we ensure the delta_patcher uses a deterministic compression step so hashes are reproducible? Please add tests for that.
Lina Ortega: Added deterministic gzip flags and an integration test that validates stable hashes across runs.
Marcus Patel: Terraform changes look good but we should gate registry-mirror behind a feature flag until 1st customer validation.
Lina Ortega: Added variable enable_registry_mirror with sensible default=false and documented upgrade steps.
Diego Ruiz: Did e2e smoke run against a coreos-flavor microk8s? Networking behaved differently in my run.
Lina Ortega: Yes we added a coreos job to the CI matrix and adjusted CNI detection logic; I also added an ops note about iptables vs nft fallback.
Ava Chen: Approved after minor comments addressed.
Marcus Patel: Approved.
Diego Ruiz: Approved.
source.testing_instructions: Create a local registry mirror and import the microk8s bundle using installer/bin/redwood-install --airgap --bundle path/to/microk8s-bundle.tar.gz --target microk8s. For Terraform: set module variable enable_registry_mirror=true and run terraform apply; verify that installer writes the local mirror config to /etc/containerd/config.toml on nodes. Run tests/tests/e2e/microk8s/smoke_test.sh against the cluster and confirm redwood pods reach Ready within 6 minutes.
source.rollback_plan: If issues are detected, run installer/bin/redwood-install --rollback --target microk8s which will revert applied k8s manifests and unregister local registry entries; Terraform changes are backwards-compatible when enable_registry_mirror=false, so revert the module version to the previous tag (v1.12.3) and run terraform apply to restore prior state.

source.title: atomic manifest stitcher and airgap compatibility layer for distro-aware bootstrap
source.author: Ava Chen
source.release_notes: Adds an atomic manifest stitcher to produce distro-aware offline bundles and improves offline bootstrap compatibility across k3s/rke2/kubeadm. Includes helm/terraform updates and new upgrade/rollback hooks.
source.body:
Title: Atomic manifest stitcher and airgap compatibility layer for distro-aware bootstrap.

Description: This PR introduces a new "manifest stitcher" tool and accompanying compatibility layer to simplify creating slim, atomic offline bundles that work across multiple Kubernetes distros (k3s, rke2, kubeadm). The stitcher consumes per-distro manifest fragments (CRDs, kubelet flags, CNI overlays) and outputs a single validated, signed and delta-friendly bundle that the private installer can apply atomically during bootstrap or upgrades. The changeset also includes Helm chart adjustments to support local-OCI registry overrides, a Terraform module to seed local registries with stitched OCI indices, and upgrade/rollback hooks to ensure safe application of delta updates in air-gapped environments.

Motivation and context: Customers deploying Redwood Private in air-gapped/on-prem environments run a diverse set of Kubernetes distros and often require minimal offline bundles that match their distro-specific runtime expectations. Prior approach produced monolithic vendor bundles that were large and hard to apply atomically. This made upgrades brittle and increased bootstrap time. The manifest stitcher makes bundles smaller (by selecting only required components), ensures distro compatibility via a lightweight compatibility matrix, and provides atomic apply semantics with an on-disk manifest manifest-lock to help rollbacks. This reduces bootstrap time and improves upgrade reliability (fewer partial-applies).

What this PR does (summary):
- Adds a new tool `installer/stitcher` (Go) that:
  - Loads per-distro manifest fragments from `manifests/overlays/*`
  - Resolves component dependencies and produces a single `stitched-manifest.yaml` and an OCI index that references per-arch images
  - Signs the stitched manifest and produces a delta-friendly index for fast incremental upgrades
  - Emits an atomic apply plan with pre/post hooks that are executed by the installer runtime
- Implements an on-disk manifest lock and rollback plan (`installer/runtime/manifestlock.go`) used by the upgrade controller
- Adds Helm chart changes to `deploy/helm/airgap` to accept `spec.localRegistry.override` and index-based image resolution
- Adds Terraform module `terraform/modules/registry-seed` to seed a local registry with stitched OCI indices and support ACLs for restricted networks
- Small improvements to existing preflight checks and docs (`docs/airgap-preflight.md`) describing distro compatibility requirements and recommended bundle sizes

Commits (high-level):
- 8b5d2f2: Add stitcher CLI skeleton and manifest fragment loader
- 3f1c7a1: Dependency graph resolver and component selector + unit tests
- a7dd9b4: OCI index emitter and signature support (cosign compatible)
- c2f9e10: Installer runtime changes: manifest lock, apply plan executor, rollback walker
- e4a7d21: Helm & Terraform integration and docs
- 9b3aaf0: Integration tests and flaky test fixes for airgap network emulation

Files changed (high level):
- installer/stitcher/main.go (new)
- installer/stitcher/resolver.go (new)
- installer/runtime/manifestlock.go (modified)
- installer/runtime/apply_executor.go (modified)
- deploy/helm/airgap/values.yaml (modified)
- deploy/helm/airgap/templates/registry-overrides.yaml (new)
- terraform/modules/registry-seed/main.tf (new)
- terraform/modules/registry-seed/outputs.tf (new)
- manifests/overlays/k3s/* (new fragments)
- manifests/overlays/rke2/* (new fragments)
- manifests/overlays/kubeadm/* (new fragments)
- docs/airgap-preflight.md (modified)
- tests/integration/airgap_stitcher_test.go (new)

Snippets (illustrative):
- Example resolver selection: `component := resolver.SelectByPolicy(arch, minimal, distro)`
- Helm values override: `localRegistry: { enabled: true, url: "localhost:5000", indexRef: "oci://localhost:5000/redwood/stitched-index:20260215" }`

Review conversation (high-level):
- Miguel Ortega (reviewer): "Nice reduction in bundle size in my local runs (~35% smaller for k3s minimal). A few nits on error wrapping and logging in resolver.go. Can we add a dry-run mode for the terraform module when the registry is not reachable?"
- Ava Chen (author): "Thanks Miguel — addressed logging nits and added `--dry-run` to the terraform module wrapper script (see scripts/registry-seed-dryrun.sh). Added integration test to assert dry-run does not push images."
- Kimberly Tran (reviewer): "Please ensure the signed manifest format is documented and that cosign verification is optional but recommended. Also add a migration note for existing customers who rely on the old monolithic bundles."
- Ava Chen (author): "Added docs section `docs/airgap-preflight.md` explaining signature verification and a migration path. Marked signature verification optional via `installer.runtime.enableSignatureVerification` flag."
- Sasha Patel (reviewer): "Integration tests flaked on first run (network emulator). Rerun CI after test timeout increase; passing now."

CI and checks:
- CI: unit-tests (Go 1.22) — pass
- CI: integration-tests/airgap (container-based network emulator) — initially failed due to a 30s timing assumption, test timeout increased and rerun passed
- CI: lint/helm — pass with minor YAML ordering warning fixed in second commit
- Security scan: SCA reported a transitive dev dependency out-of-date; pinned in go.mod and re-run — pass

Backward compatibility and migration:
- Default behaviour is backwards compatible: if no overlays are present the installer continues to use the existing monolithic vendor bundle pipeline.
- New behavior (stitcher-enabled) is opt-in via `installer.runtime.enableStitchedBundles=true`.
- Migration doc added describing how to create a compatible stitched index from an existing vendor bundle and how to perform a canary rollout with the dedicated capacity tier.

Testing and QA:
- Unit tests added for resolver logic and signature handling (coverage +6%).
- Integration tests simulate k3s and rke2 nodes pulling from a seeded local registry (CI uses a container registry shim).
- Manual acceptance: performed on private lab (baremetal) using a seeded OCI registry and kubeadm 1.27 clusters. Observed successful atomic apply and rollback when pre/post hooks triggered a deliberate failure.

Docs and changelog:
- docs/airgap-preflight.md: new section on distro compatibility matrix, signature verification, and recommended bundle sizing.
- RELEASE_NOTES.md: summarizes user-facing changes and flags.

Outstanding items and follow-ups:
- ENG-4234: Add a web UI preview for stitched bundles in Console (follow-up ticket).
- Improve heuristics for component selection to consider tokenized prompt workloads (performance follow-up).

Merge outcome: After reviewers approved and CI green, PR was squashed and merged into main. Merge commit message summarizes stitcher intent and includes the compatibility matrix excerpt.

If you want to reproduce locally: Steps (condensed):
1) go build ./installer/stitcher && ./installer/stitcher --overlay=manifests/overlays/k3s --out=./out/stitched.yaml --index=oci://localhost:5000/redwood/stitched-index:local --sign=false
2) helm upgrade --install redwood ./deploy/helm/airgap -f deploy/helm/airgap/values.yaml --set localRegistry.url=localhost:5000 --set installer.runtime.enableStitchedBundles=true
3) terraform apply -var='registry_endpoint=http://localhost:5000' terraform/modules/registry-seed

Notes: This PR intentionally keeps the cosign signature verification optional to avoid blocking customers without a KMS. Production recommendation is to enable signatures and integrate with a KMS/HSM for private deployments.

Feedback welcome — if there are additional distro edge-cases we should add a short manifest fragment and run the stitcher against a multi-distro lab to validate behavior for the next patch release.
