Title: Atomic manifest stitcher and airgap compatibility layer for distro-aware bootstrap.

Description: This PR introduces a new "manifest stitcher" tool and accompanying compatibility layer to simplify creating slim, atomic offline bundles that work across multiple Kubernetes distros (k3s, rke2, kubeadm). The stitcher consumes per-distro manifest fragments (CRDs, kubelet flags, CNI overlays) and outputs a single validated, signed and delta-friendly bundle that the private installer can apply atomically during bootstrap or upgrades. The changeset also includes Helm chart adjustments to support local-OCI registry overrides, a Terraform module to seed local registries with stitched OCI indices, and upgrade/rollback hooks to ensure safe application of delta updates in air-gapped environments.

Motivation and context: Customers deploying Redwood Private in air-gapped/on-prem environments run a diverse set of Kubernetes distros and often require minimal offline bundles that match their distro-specific runtime expectations. Prior approach produced monolithic vendor bundles that were large and hard to apply atomically. This made upgrades brittle and increased bootstrap time. The manifest stitcher makes bundles smaller (by selecting only required components), ensures distro compatibility via a lightweight compatibility matrix, and provides atomic apply semantics with an on-disk manifest manifest-lock to help rollbacks. This reduces bootstrap time and improves upgrade reliability (fewer partial-applies).

What this PR does (summary):
- Adds a new tool `installer/stitcher` (Go) that:
  - Loads per-distro manifest fragments from `manifests/overlays/*`
  - Resolves component dependencies and produces a single `stitched-manifest.yaml` and an OCI index that references per-arch images
  - Signs the stitched manifest and produces a delta-friendly index for fast incremental upgrades
  - Emits an atomic apply plan with pre/post hooks that are executed by the installer runtime
- Implements an on-disk manifest lock and rollback plan (`installer/runtime/manifestlock.go`) used by the upgrade controller
- Adds Helm chart changes to `deploy/helm/airgap` to accept `spec.localRegistry.override` and index-based image resolution
- Adds Terraform module `terraform/modules/registry-seed` to seed a local registry with stitched OCI indices and support ACLs for restricted networks
- Small improvements to existing preflight checks and docs (`docs/airgap-preflight.md`) describing distro compatibility requirements and recommended bundle sizes

Commits (high-level):
- 8b5d2f2: Add stitcher CLI skeleton and manifest fragment loader
- 3f1c7a1: Dependency graph resolver and component selector + unit tests
- a7dd9b4: OCI index emitter and signature support (cosign compatible)
- c2f9e10: Installer runtime changes: manifest lock, apply plan executor, rollback walker
- e4a7d21: Helm & Terraform integration and docs
- 9b3aaf0: Integration tests and flaky test fixes for airgap network emulation

Files changed (high level):
- installer/stitcher/main.go (new)
- installer/stitcher/resolver.go (new)
- installer/runtime/manifestlock.go (modified)
- installer/runtime/apply_executor.go (modified)
- deploy/helm/airgap/values.yaml (modified)
- deploy/helm/airgap/templates/registry-overrides.yaml (new)
- terraform/modules/registry-seed/main.tf (new)
- terraform/modules/registry-seed/outputs.tf (new)
- manifests/overlays/k3s/* (new fragments)
- manifests/overlays/rke2/* (new fragments)
- manifests/overlays/kubeadm/* (new fragments)
- docs/airgap-preflight.md (modified)
- tests/integration/airgap_stitcher_test.go (new)

Snippets (illustrative):
- Example resolver selection: `component := resolver.SelectByPolicy(arch, minimal, distro)`
- Helm values override: `localRegistry: { enabled: true, url: "localhost:5000", indexRef: "oci://localhost:5000/redwood/stitched-index:20260215" }`

Review conversation (high-level):
- Miguel Ortega (reviewer): "Nice reduction in bundle size in my local runs (~35% smaller for k3s minimal). A few nits on error wrapping and logging in resolver.go. Can we add a dry-run mode for the terraform module when the registry is not reachable?"
- Ava Chen (author): "Thanks Miguel — addressed logging nits and added `--dry-run` to the terraform module wrapper script (see scripts/registry-seed-dryrun.sh). Added integration test to assert dry-run does not push images."
- Kimberly Tran (reviewer): "Please ensure the signed manifest format is documented and that cosign verification is optional but recommended. Also add a migration note for existing customers who rely on the old monolithic bundles."
- Ava Chen (author): "Added docs section `docs/airgap-preflight.md` explaining signature verification and a migration path. Marked signature verification optional via `installer.runtime.enableSignatureVerification` flag."
- Sasha Patel (reviewer): "Integration tests flaked on first run (network emulator). Rerun CI after test timeout increase; passing now."

CI and checks:
- CI: unit-tests (Go 1.22) — pass
- CI: integration-tests/airgap (container-based network emulator) — initially failed due to a 30s timing assumption, test timeout increased and rerun passed
- CI: lint/helm — pass with minor YAML ordering warning fixed in second commit
- Security scan: SCA reported a transitive dev dependency out-of-date; pinned in go.mod and re-run — pass

Backward compatibility and migration:
- Default behaviour is backwards compatible: if no overlays are present the installer continues to use the existing monolithic vendor bundle pipeline.
- New behavior (stitcher-enabled) is opt-in via `installer.runtime.enableStitchedBundles=true`.
- Migration doc added describing how to create a compatible stitched index from an existing vendor bundle and how to perform a canary rollout with the dedicated capacity tier.

Testing and QA:
- Unit tests added for resolver logic and signature handling (coverage +6%).
- Integration tests simulate k3s and rke2 nodes pulling from a seeded local registry (CI uses a container registry shim).
- Manual acceptance: performed on private lab (baremetal) using a seeded OCI registry and kubeadm 1.27 clusters. Observed successful atomic apply and rollback when pre/post hooks triggered a deliberate failure.

Docs and changelog:
- docs/airgap-preflight.md: new section on distro compatibility matrix, signature verification, and recommended bundle sizing.
- RELEASE_NOTES.md: summarizes user-facing changes and flags.

Outstanding items and follow-ups:
- ENG-4234: Add a web UI preview for stitched bundles in Console (follow-up ticket).
- Improve heuristics for component selection to consider tokenized prompt workloads (performance follow-up).

Merge outcome: After reviewers approved and CI green, PR was squashed and merged into main. Merge commit message summarizes stitcher intent and includes the compatibility matrix excerpt.

If you want to reproduce locally: Steps (condensed):
1) go build ./installer/stitcher && ./installer/stitcher --overlay=manifests/overlays/k3s --out=./out/stitched.yaml --index=oci://localhost:5000/redwood/stitched-index:local --sign=false
2) helm upgrade --install redwood ./deploy/helm/airgap -f deploy/helm/airgap/values.yaml --set localRegistry.url=localhost:5000 --set installer.runtime.enableStitchedBundles=true
3) terraform apply -var='registry_endpoint=http://localhost:5000' terraform/modules/registry-seed

Notes: This PR intentionally keeps the cosign signature verification optional to avoid blocking customers without a KMS. Production recommendation is to enable signatures and integrate with a KMS/HSM for private deployments.

Feedback welcome — if there are additional distro edge-cases we should add a short manifest fragment and run the stitcher against a multi-distro lab to validate behavior for the next patch release.

source.title: add-auxkey-rotation-controller-and-cert-proxy-helm-terraform-integration
source.author: Maya Chen
source.release_notes: Introduce a new controller to automate staggered auxiliary key (auxkey) rotations against PKCS#11-backed HSMs and Vault transit. Adds Helm chart changes to deploy a cert-proxy sidecar and Terraform module to bind KMS policies + grant sets. Includes rolling upgrade/rollback hooks and migration helpers for sealed secrets to Vault-backed envelopes.
source.description:
Motivation: Private installer customers asked for a safer, auditable way to rotate signing keys stored in HSMs and to automate certificate refreshes without application downtime. This PR implements an auxkey rotation controller that orchestrates staged HSM key rollovers, integrates with Vault transit (or PKCS11 plugins), and provides Helm + Terraform primitives and upgrade hooks so operators can adopt automated rotations with rollback safety.

What this PR does (high level):
- Adds `signing-auxkey-controller` binary (k8s controller) that: performs staged key generation, publishes new public material to a configurable endpoint, triggers certificate reissue via the cert-proxy sidecar, and performs a coordinated switchover once signatures verify.
- Helm chart changes: a new `cert-proxy` sidecar template, pre/postupgrade hooks for key-sync and cache-warm, and a `postrender` helper to inject PKCS11 configuration for air-gapped installs.
- Terraform module `tf/kms/grantset` to create policy bindings between tenant service accounts and KMS/HSM roles (works with both cloud KMS and on-prem HSM gateways).
- Migration tool `sealed-to-vault` (lightweight CLI) to convert existing sealed-secrets blobs to Vault-wrapped keys, with a dry-run flag.
- Upgrade/rollback tooling: preflight checks, staged canary policy, and a rollback path that reverts to the previous auxkey and triggers a certificate reissue.
- End-to-end tests: small e2e harness that runs against the `mini-hsm` simulator used in CI and a manual test plan included for on-prem customers.

Checklist:
- [x] Controller core logic + unit tests
- [x] Helm templates and hooks
- [x] Terraform module and examples
- [x] Migration helper CLI + docs
- [x] E2E tests against mini-hsm simulator
- [x] Security review and threat model notes attached (ENG-6128)

Commits summary:
- feat(controller): initial auxkey rotation controller scaffold and reconciliation loop
- feat(helm): add cert-proxy sidecar, hooks, and values schema updates
- feat(tf): add kms grantset module and example binding for installer cluster role
- feat(migration): sealed-to-vault CLI with --dry-run and --apply
- test(e2e): add mini-hsm e2e job and CI workflow integration
- docs: key-ceremony plan, operator guide, rollback steps

Files changed (high level):
- cmd/signing-auxkey-controller/main.go (new)
- internal/rotations/* (controller reconciliation + helpers)
- charts/installer/templates/_cert-proxy.tpl (new), templates/hooks/pre-upgrade-key-sync.yaml (new), values.schema.json (updated)
- terraform/kms/grantset/main.tf (new), examples/grantset/README.md (new)
- tools/sealed-to-vault/main.go (new)
- test/e2e/mini-hsm/* (new harness and fixtures)
- docs/operator/key-ceremony.md (new), docs/operator/rollback-guide.md (updated)

Review thread summary: 
- Diego Alvarez: asked for stronger idempotency guarantees around the controller's switchover path and requested an explicit leader-election backstop to avoid dual-active signing. Noted this was critical for multi-AZ installs.
- Maya Chen (author): responded with design note and added leader-election via existing controller-runtime leader election; added a `switchoverLock` sanity check to ensure only one controller instance performs final cutover.
- Priya Natarajan: requested test coverage for the migration CLI and that the CLI refuse to apply conversions when the Vault config is incomplete. Author added dry-run and validation checks plus unit tests.
- Elliot Brooks: security review asked to document audit events emitted and to ensure that private key material never leaves the HSM path in logs. Author added structured events (no key material) and sanitized logging.
- CI: initial run showed flaky e2e due to mini-hsm timing; adjusted probe timings and re-ran, which passed. One lint failure for helm schema was fixed in a follow-up commit.
- Review outcome: Diego approved after leader-election change, Priya approved after tests added, Elliot gave final security approval.

CI summary: initial failure (flaky e2e) -> fix timing -> rerun -> all checks passed (unit, lint, helm-chart-lint, e2e-mini-hsm). Integration tests passed with a note about increasing the default probe timeout for air-gapped environments.

Operational notes / runbook extract: 
- Default rotation interval: off by default. Operator can enable via values: rotations.enabled=true and configure `rotationWindow` and `canaryPercentage`.
- Rollback: controller stores previous auxkey fingerprint in a k8s secret `rotation-history/<name>` and will attempt automated rebind; if automated rollback fails, run `sealed-to-vault --rollback --name <release>` to restore envelopes.
- Audit: controller emits events to k8s audit sink and writes structured entries to the installer log (no private material).

Migration guidance: run `sealed-to-vault --dry-run` and inspect output; apply only after Vault transit path and KMS grants are verified. Example Terraform snippet included in examples/grantset.

Security considerations: the controller supports two KMS backends: Vault transit and PKCS11 HSM gateway. PKCS11 requires a secure mount and the Helm `postrender` hook ensures the node-level PKCS11 config is injected only when `values.hsm.enabled=true`. The controller enforces a maximum age for auxkeys and requires operator acknowledgement for rotations that cross account boundaries.

Why this is different from previous rotation work: existing PRs focused on rotating primary signer keys or automating unseal. This change introduces a short-lived auxiliary signing key pattern (auxkey) to make rollovers less risky, plus a cert-proxy sidecar to decouple certificate issuance from application lifecycle and explicit Terraform grantsets to bridge cloud and on-prem KMS semantics.

Follow-ups:
- ENG-6135: expand e2e matrix to test hardware-backed HSMs in CI lab (next sprint)
- Add Prometheus metrics for rotation state transitions (PR follow-up planned)

Merge: Squash-merged into main on 2026-02-26 after final approvals.

source.title: sealed-bundle-registry-hash-rotation-and-helm-overlay-transformer
source.author: Maya Chen
source.ci_status: build: pass; unit: pass; integ: pass; e2e-airgap: flaky->rerun->pass
source.description:
Motivation: Improve on-prem sealed-bundle upgrades in air-gapped environments by enabling atomic registry hash rotation and providing a Helm overlay transformer that rewrites chart image references to a local seeded registry. This addresses two common failure modes: (1) operator mismatch when bundle image digests change during incremental upgrades, and (2) manual, error-prone sed-based image rewrites during preseed operations.

What this PR does: Adds a registry hash rotation mechanism to the offline bundle layout and a Helm overlay transformer used by the installer preflight and apply phases. The registry rotation ensures sealed-bundle manifests and OCI index entries can be remapped atomically to a new local registry prefix using a rotation map file. The Helm overlay transformer is a small, deterministic engine that rewrites Helm values/image fields and Chart.lock entries to point at the local registry and validates digest availability in the seeded registry before applying charts.

Key components changed: (1) offline/bundle/index-rotator.go: implements rotation map parsing, index remapping, and checksum update for sealed bundles; (2) installer/helm/overlay-transformer: new binary used by the private-installer to rewrite charts during apply; (3) terraform/modules/registry-seed: added output for rotation-map.json and hooks to upload the map to the preseed registry; (4) scripts/upgrade/rollback-hook.sh: improved rollback to consult previous rotation snapshot and revert index pointers atomically; (5) kustomize/overlays/airgap: sample overlay demonstrating integration.

Upgrade path: New bundles produced by our release tooling include a rotation-map.json alongside the OCI index (examples included in tools/release). When operators run an upgrade, the installer uses the rotation map to mirror any new digests into the local registry and updates the local index metadata. Existing customers without the rotation map will fall back to legacy mirror behavior (no rotation), preserving backwards compatibility.

Rollback semantics: The installer records a rotation snapshot (rotation-snapshot.yaml) in the cluster namespace before making changes. The rollback-hook consults this snapshot and reverses the index remapping if needed; it also uses Helm's --atomic flag and waits for chart post-install probes to avoid partial application.

Security and audit: Rotation maps are signed using the existing bundle signing key (tools/sign-bundle) and validated by the installer. The overlay transformer enforces an allowlist of image registries and refuses to rewrite to registries not present in the rotation map or Terraform seed outputs. Audit events are emitted to the installer audit log for rotation apply/rollback events.

Testing and verification: Unit tests for index rotation and transformer rewrite rules are included (installer/helm/overlay-transformer/*_test.go). Integration tests were added to the airgap e2e harness to validate a staged rotation (seed registry A -> seed registry B) and an abort+rollback path. On our CI the e2e-airgap job was flaky at first due to timing of the local registry readiness; I added retry/backoff in the seed job and the flakiness was resolved.

Checklist: [x] implement rotator + transformer [x] tests added [x] terraform outputs updated [x] docs: add upgrade notes in docs/airgap/rotation.md [x] presubmit: e2e airgap green after rerun

Backwards compatibility: Designs intentionally preserve legacy mirror semantics when no rotation map is present. No changes required to existing customer Terraform manifests unless they opt into rotation (the new terraform module outputs are opt-in).

Notes for operators: See docs/airgap/rotation.md for sample rotation-map.json format, signer workflow, and a recommended two-step: mirror->verify->apply procedure for critical production clusters.
source.commits:
1) add index rotator and rotation map validation
2) add helm overlay transformer binary and tests
3) update terraform module to emit rotation-map output and upload helper
4) add rollback hook and kustomize sample overlay
5) docs: add rotation operator guide and examples
6) fix integ test timing flakiness (retry/backoff)
source.changed_files: Highlights: offline/bundle/index-rotator.go (new), installer/helm/overlay-transformer/main.go (new), installer/helm/overlay-transformer/rewrite.go (new), installer/helm/overlay-transformer/allowlist.go (new), terraform/modules/registry-seed/outputs.tf (modified), terraform/modules/registry-seed/scripts/upload-rotation.sh (new), scripts/upgrade/rollback-hook.sh (modified), docs/airgap/rotation.md (new), kustomize/overlays/airgap/rotation-sample.yaml (new), Makefile (targets for build/ci), ci/jobs/e2e-airgap.yaml (retry tweak). No large binary blobs committed; bundle examples in tools/release are small JSON manifests.
source.review_thread:
Diego: 'Can the rotator operate offline if the local registry has missing blobs?' -> Author: 'Yes, rotator will attempt to mirror missing digests into the seeded registry before remapping; if mirroring fails the apply phase aborts with a clear error and logs missing digests.'
Priya: 'Please ensure rotation-map signatures are validated with existing keyring and reject unsigned maps.' -> Author: 'Added signature validation and unit tests; installer fails fast on unsigned/invalid maps.'
Ethan: 'Concern about race between mirroring and helm apply on large bundles.' -> Author: 'Added snapshot write and logic to wait for registry manifest availability; also added exponential backoff in seed jobs.'
Reviewers: approved after small nits fixed (go vet, lint).
source.release_notes: Operators can now perform atomic registry hash rotations for sealed bundles and use the Helm overlay transformer to rewrite image refs to a local seeded registry. Opt-in via terraform module outputs; signed rotation maps required. Includes rollback snapshot support and documentation.
source.notes_for_qa: Run the e2e-airgap job with sample rotation-map.json: 1) seed registry A with initial bundle, 2) produce rotated bundle and seed registry B, 3) run installer with rotation-map pointing to registry B, 4) validate rotated images exist in local registry and charts deploy, 5) simulate failure and verify rollback snapshot reverts index mappings.
source.pilot_customer_feedback: Initial pilot with one enterprise customer (banking vertical) showed successful atomic rotation during a security-mandated image digest refresh. Customer asked for clearer rollback status in the console; added audit event and docs update.
source.changelog_entry: docs: airgap rotation and helm overlay transformer; infra: rotation-map output for registry seed module; installer: rotation and rollback snapshot support

title: sealed-bundle-registry-hash-rotation-and-helm-overlay-transformer
preview:
Motivation: Improve on-prem sealed-bundle upgrades in air-gapped environments by enabling atomic registry hash rotation and providing a Helm overlay transformer that rewrites chart image references to a local seeded registry. This addresses two common failure modes: (1) operator mismatch when bundle image digests change during incremental upgrades, and (2) manual, error-prone sed-based image rewrites during preseed operations.

What this PR does: Adds a registry hash rotation mechanism to the offline bundle layout and a Helm overlay transformer used by the installer preflight and apply phases. The registry rotation ensures sealed-bundle manifests and OCI index entries can be remapped atomically to a new local registry prefix using a rotation map file. The Helm overlay transformer is a small, deterministic engine that rewrites Helm values/image fields and Chart.lock entries to point at the local registry and validates digest availability in the seeded registry before applying charts.

Key components changed: (1) offline/bundle/index-rotator.go: implements rotation map parsing, index remapping, and checksum update for sealed bundles; (2) installer/helm/overlay-transformer: new binary used by the private-installer to rewrite charts during apply; (3) terraform/modules/registry-seed: added output for rotation-map.json and hooks to upload the map to the preseed registry; (4) scripts/upgrade/rollback-hook.sh: improved rollback to consult previous rotation snapshot and revert index pointers atomically; (5) kustomize/overlays/airgap: sample overlay demonstrating integration.

Upgrade path: New bundles produced by our release tooling include a rotation-map.json alongside the OCI index (examples included in tools/release). When operators run an upgrade, the installer uses the rotation map to mirror any new digests into the local registry and updates the local index metadata. Existing customers without the rotation map will fall back to legacy mirror behavior (no rotation), preserving backwards compatibility.

Rollback semantics: The installer records a rotation snapshot (rotation-snapshot.yaml) in the cluster namespace before making changes. The rollback-hook consults this snapshot and reverses the index remapping if needed; it also uses Helm's --atomic flag and waits for chart post-install probes to avoid partial application.

Security and audit: Rotation maps are signed using the existing bundle signing key (tools/sign-bundle) and validated by the installer. The overlay transformer enforces an allowlist of image registries and refuses to rewrite to registries not present in the rotation map or Terraform seed outputs. Audit events are emitted to the installer audit log for rotation apply/rollback events.

Testing and verification: Unit tests for index rotation and transformer rewrite rules are included (installer/helm/overlay-transformer/*_test.go). Integration tests were added to the airgap e2e harness to validate a staged rotation (seed registry A -> seed registry B) and an abort+rollback path. On our CI the e2e-airgap job was flaky at first due to timing of the local registry readiness; I added retry/backoff in the seed job and the flakiness was resolved.

Checklist: [x] implement rotator + transformer [x] tests added [x] terraform outputs updated [x] docs: add upgrade notes in docs/airgap/rotation.md [x] presubmit: e2e airgap green after rerun

Backwards compatibility: Designs intentionally preserve legacy mirror semantics when no rotation map is present. No changes required to existing customer Terraform manifests unless they opt into rotation (the new terraform module outputs are opt-in).

Notes for operators: See docs/airgap/rotation.md for sample rotation-map.json format, signer workflow, and a recommended two-step: mirror->verify->apply procedure for critical production clusters.

source.title: prevent-downgrades-and-standardize-on-disk-backup-format
source.author: Maya K. Patel
source.description:
Motivation: Past upgrades have exposed two recurring problems for private deployments: accidental downgrades (operator error or rollback scripts that do not account for schema/feature flags) and inconsistent backup formats across installer versions which makes restores brittle. This PR introduces a lightweight downgrade-blocker in the installer, pins on-disk backup metadata to a stable v2 format, and adds a deterministic restore test to the CI matrix.

Summary of changes: Added a check in the private installer that refuses to apply a release with an older semantic version if that release would require a schema downgrade; the check is driven by a new release-metadata.json (version + schema_version + migration_path). Implemented a stable on-disk backup v2 format (single tar.gz with manifests/etcd-snapshots/secrets.enc and a compact manifest.json) and utilities to produce/consume it. Introduced an idempotent schema-migration runner with pre/post hooks and automatic dry-run mode. Hooked backup/restore smoke into CI to validate cross-version restores (main->v1.3 pin and back). Updated terraform and helm manifests to pin provider/module versions used by the installer so backups produced during restore tests are reproducible.

What this change does (concrete): 1) installer/upgrade/downgrade_blocker.go: adds logic that compares incoming release metadata to cluster state and rejects operations that would lower schema_version or remove required migrations. 2) installer/backup/format/v2_spec.md + tools/backup/create_v2_backup.go + tools/backup/restore_v2.go: define and implement the v2 backup format, including an encrypted secrets container compatible with KMS/HSM integration. 3) installer/migrations/runner.go: idempotent migrations with a dry-run flag and a deterministic lock file to prevent concurrent migration runs. 4) terraform/modules/version_pins.tf and charts/private/values.yaml: version pins for terraform providers and helm images used by the private installer. 5) ci/restore-e2e.yml: new CI matrix job which takes a v1.2 backup and attempts to restore it onto this branch and then back to v1.2 to validate downgrade guard behavior.

Testing performed: unit tests for migration runner (mocks for DB state), integration restore e2e that runs in GH Actions using a small ephemeral K3s cluster and mocked KMS provider, manual end-to-end restore/upgrade tests against a local vm cluster. All CI jobs passing in the PR run after a transient failure in the first integration run (flaky network to GH container registry) which was rerun.

Rollout plan: merge to main, cut v1.3.0-rc with the metadata file included. Default installer behavior remains conservative: the downgrade-blocker is enabled by default but can be temporarily relaxed with a documented admin flag --allow-downgrade-forced (requires explicit acceptance in audit logs). We will roll out the change to Dedicated customers on a staged schedule and provide a migration guide.

Rollback plan: installer will refuse to downgrade automatically; to recover a cluster that must be moved back to an older release we provide a controlled path: 1) take a v2 backup, 2) use a provided compatibility tool to map schema changes (installer/migrations/compatibility_mapper.go), 3) run restore with --force-downgrade (requires operator approval and generates a mandatory audit bundle). The force path is intentionally manual and logged.

Security notes: secrets in backups are encrypted using envelope encryption; restore tooling integrates with KMS via the existing KMS adapter. The downgrade blocker adds an operator-visible audit event.

Related: ENG-1182 (upgrade reliability), PM-431 (customer ask: guaranteed restore across minor versions).

Checklist: [x] migration runner unit tests, [x] backup/restore e2e, [x] helm/terraform pinning, [x] docs and upgrade guide, [x] audit events for forced-downgrade.
commits: c1f4b8a: add downgrade blocker framework and unit tests
d9a2c1b: implement backup v2 create/restore and spec doc
e3179f4: idempotent migration runner and compatibility mapper
b5f02d7: pin terraform/helm/provider and update scripts
f7c89b2: add e2e restore CI job and docs
review_comments: Jonah Park: The downgrade blocker looks good but add a clear error message that points to the upgrade guide and the forced path.
Maya K. Patel: Addressed — added user-facing error text and a link to docs/upgrade-guide.md in the code path.
Sofia Ruiz: Can we make the backup format backward-compatible with some older helpers? Recommend a small shim to read v1 manifests.
Maya K. Patel: Added a thin v1->v2 adapter in tools/backup/compat_shim.go and unit tests covering edge cases.
Ben Carter: Flagging potential race in migration runner when multiple controller replicas run migrations concurrently.
Maya K. Patel: Added a deterministic migration lock file and a leader-check path; documented the invariant in runner.go comments.
SRE (CI): initial integration job failed on network to GHCR; reran and all checks passed.
source.release_notes: Installer: refuse schema-downgrades by default; introduce on-disk backup v2 format for consistent restore compatibility; idempotent migration runner with dry-run mode; provider/module version pinning for reproducible restores.
source.notes_for_ops: After merging, notify Dedicated customers of the stricter downgrade guard. Backups created by v1.2 older installers will continue to be restored via the provided v1->v2 shim for the next three minor releases. If a customer truly needs to force a downgrade, follow the documented forced-downgrade path and collect the audit bundle.

source.title: automation: multi-AZ failover + restore-drill runbooks-as-code for private installer
source.author: Asha Patel
commits: feat(ha): add terraform module for multi-az subnet groups and cross-az LB config
chore(installer): expose --drill-runbook flag to private-installer CLI
feat(helm): add values schema for zoneAffinity and failoverPriority
docs(runbooks): add runbooks-as-code YAML templates and restore playbooks
test(drill): add integration test that simulates AZ outage and validates failover
source.description:
Motivation: Private customer deployments (VPC/on-prem) need automated, testable HA and DR runbooks. This PR introduces orchestration and configuration changes that enable multi-AZ deployments, automated failover testing (restore drills), and runbooks-as-code so runbooks can be versioned and executed by the installer.

What this PR does: Adds a reusable Terraform module for multi-AZ subnet groups and load-balancer failover configuration; extends the private-installer CLI with a --drill-runbook flag to execute runbooks-as-code against a cluster; introduces Helm values to control zone affinity and priority-based pod scheduling for graceful cross-AZ failover; and adds YAML runbook templates with step-by-step, idempotent tasks to run restore drills and validate recovery.

Design notes: The Terraform module uses provider-aware subnet mapping to ensure each AZ gets isolated subnets and a cross-AZ LB with health-check-driven routing policies. Helm changes add a zoneAffinity helper template and a failoverPriority annotation applied to critical services. Runbooks are encoded as declarative YAML (sequence of actions) that the installer interprets and applies in a dry-run or live mode. Each action supports retry/backoff and post-check probes.

Rollback and safety: All runbook actions are designed to be reversible; the installer collects a snapshot prior to drills (CR/State export) and stores it in the configured object store. The runbook DSL includes a mandatory preflight check step that validates quorum and storage access. A fail-open flag is intentionally disabled by default.

Testing and validation: Added an integration test harness that can simulate: (1) AZ failure by filtering routes and marking nodes NotReady, (2) traffic shift validation using lightweight synthetic load, and (3) node restore and data re-synchronization checks. CI runs e2e-drill-sim to exercise the installer's runbook runner in a k3s multi-AZ emulation environment.

Operational runbook summary (short):
- preflight: verify subnets, LB health, KMS access, snapshot storage
- simulate-az-failure: cordon nodes in AZ, withdraw routes, confirm traffic drops to primary region
- trigger-failover: adjust LB routing weights and annotate services with failoverPriority to force leader election where applicable
- validate: run smoke tests against API, verify KV cache warm and token latency within SLOs
- rollback/restore: execute stored snapshot restore, uncordon nodes, reintroduce network paths

Files of interest: terraform/modules/network/multi_az.tf (subnet mapping), helm/charts/redwood/templates/affinity.yaml (zone-aware pod scheduling), installer/pkg/drill/runbook_templates.yaml (runbook-as-code examples), docs/runbooks/multi-az-failover.md (operator-facing guide).

Related: ENG-3249 (HA automation), ENG-3310 (private-installer runbooks).
review_comments: Marcus Lin: left a few nits on the Helm affinity template; prefer explicit topologyKeys instead of relying on default labels. Also request an example values.yaml in the chart README.
Asha Patel (author): addressed Helm nits in a follow-up commit and added example values to helm/charts/redwood/README.md.
Elena Gomez: asked for clarity on snapshot retention/cleanup during repeated drills. Recommend adding retention config and a cleanup job.
Asha Patel (author): added terraform/vars.tf with snapshot_retention_days and implemented a cleanup CronJob template in helm chart.
source.release_notes: Add multi-AZ automation modules and runbooks-as-code for private deployments. Users can now run automated restore drills via the installer (see docs/runbooks/multi-az-failover.md).
source.test_plan: Unit tests for runbook parser + integration drill simulation in CI. Manual DR drill performed in staging on 2026-02-20; post-drill verification captured in runbook-run logs (artifacts attached).
source.post_merge_actions: Backfill Private deployment quickstart with multi-AZ example, schedule a run-the-drill workshop with solutions engineering, monitor first two customer-private deployments for automated drill behavior.
source.notes_for_ops: Enable snapshot storage (S3/GCS/Onprem object store) and set snapshot_retention_days before running drills. Ensure KMS keys are accessible by installer service account.

source.title: add kubeadm vendor bundle + local registry-proxy for air-gapped installs
source.author: Maya Patel
source.ci_status: pass (linux-integration, smoke-e2e, lint) with one flaky e2e rerun resolved
source.description: Motivation: Customers running on-prem with kubeadm and strict air-gap constraints requested a self-contained vendor bundle and a small registry-proxy to bootstrap image syncs from operator media. This PR introduces a kubeadm-targeted offline bundle layout, a lightweight registry-proxy that serves the bundled images locally and a set of install-time validators and checksums. The changes aim to reduce manual steps for operators and provide a repeatable install/upgrade flow for kubeadm clusters in disconnected environments. High level changes: 1) bundles/kubeadm/: new bundle layout with images/, manifests/, checksums.txt and a manifest-catalog.json used by the installer to map images to components. 2) installer/airgap/registry-proxy: Dockerfile + minimal Go-based proxy that exposes a small HTTP API to list available images and perform staged pulls into the cluster registry (supports TLS cert injection and basic auth). 3) installer/validators: a kubeadm-specific preflight validator that verifies CRI runtime, kernel settings and bundle integrity via SHA256 checksums. 4) charts/redwood: Helm chart additions to optionally deploy the registry-proxy as a DaemonSet for single-node bootstrap and a ConfigMap-driven imageElectionPolicy for multi-node scenarios. 5) terraform/modules/airgap: a small module to provision a bastion with the proxy in customer VPCs for hybrid installs. 6) docs/airgap-kubeadm.md: step-by-step offline install and upgrade instructions plus rollback guidance and known limitations. The description and checks: implemented checksum verification for every image file, added signature-verification hook (GPG optional), and added conservative defaults for resource requests/limits for the proxy. Testing: unit tests for the proxy image list API, integration smoke tests using kind that simulate disconnected pulls by serving the bundle from a local HTTP server, and full e2e on a small kubeadm VM in our lab. Upgrade path: installer supports in-place upgrades by validating bundle manifests and applying a canary rollout to the registry-proxy and controller deployments; rollback uses Helm rollback of redwood chart and a bundle-rollback script that removes newly loaded images and restores old image tags in the registry. Checklist: [x] bundle layout and sample bundle [x] registry-proxy implementation [x] preflight validators [x] Helm chart toggles and values [x] Terraform module + examples [x] documentation and examples [x] CI smoke tests and e2e run Notes: This PR is intentionally conservative on the proxy feature set (no dynamic mirroring rules yet). Follow-ups are planned to add automated mirror sync tooling and OCI index support. Related: ENG-5128 for product acceptance tests and SUP-789 for customer support case that motivated the work.
commits: init: add kubeadm bundle layout and sample manifests
feat(registry-proxy): add minimal HTTP proxy and image listing API
chore(installer): add preflight validator for kubeadm airgap installs
feat(charts): helm toggles to deploy registry-proxy and imageElectionPolicy
infra(terraform): add airgap bastion module and example usage
docs: add airgap-kubeadm install and upgrade guide
test: add kind-based smoke test for bundle install
ci: include bundle validation in lint pipeline
review_comments: Diego Ramos: Can the proxy support TLS with custom cert files from the bundle? Also request adding a readiness probe to the daemonset.
Author(Maya Patel): Proxy accepts certs via /etc/registry-proxy/certs mounted from the bundle. Added readinessProbe and default 5s interval in manifest. Documented cert placement in README.
Ava Chen: The preflight validator exits with code 1 on missing kernel params but we should surface a non-fatal warning option for eval-only runs.
Author: Added --strict flag to the validator and retained current behavior as strict by default. Updated docs to show eval-only mode.
Samir Kulkarni: Terraform module should not create public endpoints by default; changed to create private-only bastion and added an example with NAT gateway options.
Author: Updated module variables and examples, default set to private_subnet = true.
test_matrix: integration: kind-based bundle install smoke - pass
e2e: kubeadm VM lab run - pass (small cluster, 3 nodes)
source.release_notes: Add kubeadm-targeted offline bundle and lightweight local registry-proxy to simplify air-gapped installs. Includes preflight validators, Helm toggles, Terraform module for bastion provisioning, and docs for install/upgrade/rollback.
source.upgrade_notes: To upgrade an existing airgap kubeadm install, prepare a new bundle with manifests/ checksums and upload to the bastion. Run the installer with --bundle-path pointing to the new bundle; installer will validate checksums, apply manifest diffs and perform a canary rollout for the registry-proxy before upgrading controllers. See docs/airgap-kubeadm.md for step-by-step commands.
source.rollback_instructions: If the upgrade causes regressions, run helm rollback release redwood --namespace redwood to restore previous controller state, then run scripts/bundle-rollback.sh on the bastion to purge newly loaded images and restore registry tags. Consult logs/artifacts in /var/log/redwood-installer for audit trail.
source.notes: This PR targets kubeadm workflows and intentionally avoids adding RKE2/PKS-specific code. Follow-up PRs will add OCI index support and automated mirror sync agents.

source.title: add offline distro image-indexing + delta snapshot tooling for safe on-prem upgrades
source.author: Maya Chen
source.description: Motivation: customers running air-gapped/private deployments require deterministic offline upgrade artifacts and a compact index that maps distro variants and kube runtimes to vendored OCI images. This PR adds a stand-alone image indexer and delta snapshot generator to the offline installer toolchain so operators can produce minimal upgrade bundles and validated rollback snapshots without access to public registries. Summary: implements an image indexing service (cli and library), a delta snapshot format (signed manifest + OCI manifest refs), Helm chart changes to consume indexed manifests at install/upgrade time, Terraform module updates for offline bundle storage metadata, and a small orchestration helper that validates rollback compatibility before applying upgrades. Design: image-indexer scans a local tarball of mirrored OCI layers or a registry mirror endpoint and emits a compact JSON index keyed by distro profile (ubuntu-20.04 / almalinux-8 / sles-15), kubelet/kernel pairing, and runtime variant (containerd/cri-o). The delta snapshot builder computes diffs against a target bundle and emits a signed snapshot with three sections: additions, removals, and pinned-image-shas. Signatures use the existing installer signing keychain (supports KMS-backed signing). The Helm chart change introduces a new values subtree offline.imageIndex.path and a small initContainer that pulls the indexed manifests into the local registry mirror on first boot. Terraform: module offline-bundles now exposes a metadata.json that contains index version, distro tags and a preflight hash used by the installer to gate upgrades. Rollback safety: the orchestration helper runs a dry-run verification against the index to ensure no required images are missing and that KV cache schema changes are compatible with the target runtime. Notes on security: image index and delta snapshots are signed and verified; index generation validates manifest digests against vendored layers to avoid TOCTOU failures. Backwards compatibility: installer will fall back to previous behavior if offline.imageIndex is not provided. Testing: unit tests for indexer hashing and snapshot diff, integration test that simulates a k3s and rke2 upgrade using vendored images, and an airgap e2e that verifies rollback snapshot application. Rollout checklist: 1) publish new installer binary in release channel, 2) run compatibility sweep on existing customer bundles, 3) communicate upgrade and rollback validation steps to SRE. See linked Linear tasks for follow-up: ENG-2039 (index rotation), ENG-2104 (metric + telemetry), and ENG-2156 (support for bottlerocket).
source.commit_summary: 3 commits: add image-indexer cli and library; delta snapshot builder + signing; helm + terraform wiring and integration tests
review_comments: Ethan Park (2026-02-19): Nice work. Two questions: 1) how do you expect operators to rotate index versions; 2) do we expose telemetry for missing-image errors? Please add unit tests for the index hash function.

Maya Chen (2026-02-20): Response: added index rotation notes under ENG-2039 and wired a preflight-metric that increments on missing-image detection. Added unit tests for hashing and a round-trip test against a small tarball (see test/indexer_test.go).
Sofia Martinez (2026-02-21): Found a failure in the CI run where the helm template job failed because values.schema.json rejected the new subtree. Suggest relaxing schema to allow legacy behavior.
Maya Chen (2026-02-22): Fixed schema to accept absent offline.imageIndex and added integration test to cover legacy path.
CI Bot (2026-02-23): build: fail, helm-template: fail, unit-tests: pass, integration-tests: pending
CI Bot (2026-02-24): build: pass, helm-template: pass, unit-tests: pass, integration-tests: pass
Arjun Rao (2026-02-24): Approving after CI green. Left a minor comment about logging verbosity for indexer in debug builds.
Maya Chen (2026-02-25): Addressed logging comment and squashed commits for merge. Added release notes entry.
source.release_notes: Adds offline image indexing and delta snapshot tooling for the private installer. Enables compact upgrade bundles, signed rollback snapshots, and a Helm init hook to populate local mirrors from index manifests. Backwards compatible when index not supplied.

source.title: staged-rollback-with-dependency-graph-and-state-seed-preservation
source.author: Asha Mehta
source.description: Context: customers running Private deployments reported rare failures when performing multi-stage upgrades where component inter-dependencies (schema, config, seed data) required coordinated rollbacks. Motivation: provide a safe, testable staged rollback path that understands service dependency ordering and preserves tenant-visible seeded state so that partial rollbacks do not leave the cluster in an inconsistent or unbootable condition. What this PR does: 1) Introduces a reversible dependency graph used by the installer to compute rollback stages; edges cover DB migrations, config map velocity, helm chart ordering, and tenant seed/data artifacts. 2) Adds ‘seed-preserve’ mode: when enabled, pre-rollback hooks snapshot tenant-seeded objects (application-level seeds and lightweight KV entries) into encrypted, versioned manifests stored in object storage with a TTL and rotation policy. 3) Implements a staged rollback executor that runs rollback stages in reverse topological order with signal checkpoints so operators can pause between stages and run integration smoke tests. 4) Adds a cli subcommand “installer rollback-plan --dry-run” that emits a human-readable plan and an operator-safe JSON plan; the JSON includes per-stage healthchecks and required preconditions. 5) Adds automatic downgrade guard checks that prevent application binary downgrades if schema compatibility checks fail; these checks are pluggable to allow product teams to opt-in. 6) Adds telemetry events and RBAC-scoped audit logs for each rollback stage and seed snapshot operation. Checklist: [x] design doc linked (Linear: ENG-2345) [x] unit tests for plan generator and serializer [x] integration test harness for staged executor using local Vagrant cluster [x] end-to-end dry-run validated against a sample two-tenant cluster [x] documented operational playbook and CLI usage (docs/installer/rollback.md) Testing notes: dry-run and encrypted snapshot creation were exercised against a local postgres instance and minio; tests include simulated partial failure (post-stage failure) and observed automatic resume/retry behavior. Compatibility: default behavior is opt-out; installer flags preserved for existing installs. Rollout plan: feature behind feature flag enabled by default for new private installs, and behind opt-in for existing customers; will be gated by a 2-week beta with selected customers. Rollback plan: staged executor supports resume-from-stage and manual abort; seed-preserve manifests can be used to restore tenant seeds if automated rollback cannot complete. Related work: complements earlier safe-rollback and migration-preview tooling. Security: snapshots are encrypted with customer-provided KMS keys when available; rotation TTL of 30 days by default. Limitations: not targeted at large binary data restores (those remain outside scope); heavy tenant-count clusters may need longer pause windows. Migration path: installer will validate cluster state and optionally recommend a canary rollback before full rollback. Links: design doc https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-2345, sample plan output in PR files. Commit summary: a) add dependency-graph package and graph serialization; b) implement plan generator and JSON emitter; c) implement staged executor with pause/resume; d) seed snapshotter and encryption layer; e) CLI flags and documentation; f) integration tests and telemetry hooks; g) unit tests and mocks. Files changed (high level): installer/cmd/rollback.go; installer/pkg/depgraph/* (new); installer/pkg/seedshot/* (new); installer/pkg/executor/staged.go; docs/installer/rollback.md; charts/redwood/values.rollback.yaml; scripts/e2e/rollback-smoke.sh; proto/installer/audit.proto; Makefile updates. Review conversation summary: initial review requested a smaller default TTL and clearer UI for pause/resume. Connor: "Please make TTL configurable and ensure KMS fallback behavior is explicit in logs." Author: "Made TTL configurable via --snapshot-ttl and added KMS fallback log lines; tests updated." Mateo: "Executor should surface stage-level metrics for SLOs." Author: "Added basic counters and trace spans; will iterate on dashboards in a follow-up." Sana: "Does this change the default behavior for existing installs?" Author: "No, default opt-out for upgrades; new installs get opt-in true but flagged in release notes." CI: pipeline ran unit, lint, and integration jobs; one flaky e2e job failed initially and was re-run; final CI green. Merge outcome: squash-merged to main after approvals and green CI. Release notes: "Added staged rollback support with dependency-aware execution and optional seed-state preservation. New CLI tools: installer rollback-plan and installer rollback --resume. Snapshots are encrypted with KMS when configured. Opt-in for existing clusters."

Release notes: "Added staged rollback support with dependency-aware execution and optional seed-state preservation. New CLI tools: installer rollback-plan and installer rollback --resume. Snapshots are encrypted with KMS when configured. Opt-in for existing clusters."

source.title: Control-plane subnet affinity policy and conditional DNS proxying for cross-VPC control plane
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.release_notes: Adds a zone-aware affinity allocator for control-plane subnets with a conditional DNS proxying component to allow cross-VPC resolver handoff. Backwards compatible; opt-in policy via installer config. Improves control-plane stability during AZ drain and multi-account setups.
source.description: Context: Customers running Redwood Private across multiple VPCs and accounts have seen control-plane flakiness when control-plane endpoints and control-plane instances are scattered across AZs and when cross-VPC DNS forwarding is not consistently available. This PR implements a deterministic affinity policy for allocating control-plane subnets (prefer stickiness to preferred AZs and capacity pools) and adds a conditional DNS proxying helper that automates resolver handoff for hosted/private route scenarios. Motivation: reduce cross-AZ control-plane chattiness, avoid hot-spotting of ephemeral endpoints during rolling upgrades, and make DNS handoff deterministic for cross-account control-plane peering. What changed (high level): 1) Introduce a subnet-affinity policy module used by the installer to pick or create control-plane subnets based on zone scores, capacity tags, and historical allocations. The allocator prefers keeping existing allocations stable during upgrades unless an explicit "rebalance" flag is set. 2) Add a lightweight dns-proxy-controller that can be optionally enabled in the control-plane VPC; the controller programs conditional forward rules (Route 53 Resolver rules or on-prem DNS proxy entries) to point consumer VPCs to the nearest control-plane endpoint IPs and includes TTL-based failover. 3) Security hardening: automatic security-group templates for control-plane endpoints that limit port exposure, explicit IAM policy additions for the DNS controller to manage resolver rules, and audit log hooks. 4) Installer CLI flags and Helm values: new flags --subnet-affinity-mode (off|preferred|strict), --dns-proxy-enabled (true|false), and helm values under controlplane.affinity and controlplane.dnsProxy. 5) Tests and infra: unit tests for allocator heuristics, an integration harness using localstack for resolver rule emulation, and Terraform module updates to surface the new options. Rollout plan: feature is off by default. Admins can opt-in via installer config; rolling upgrade path preserves current allocations unless --subnet-affinity-mode=strict is specified. Checklist: - [x] Design doc (linked in ENG-1973) - [x] Terraform module changes - [x] Helm chart values and templates - [x] Unit & integration tests - [x] E2E smoke in staging - [x] Docs updates (quickstart/upgrade notes) Backwards compatibility: installing without opt-in preserves existing behavior. Strict affinity mode may reallocate subnets if explicitly requested by operators. See release_notes for upgrade guidance. Related: ENG-1973, PM-381, SUP-4421.
commits: a1b2c3d: add subnet affinity allocator and policy structs
b2c3d4e: wire allocator into installer create/upgrade flows
c3d4e5f: add dns-proxy-controller and RBAC bindings
d4e5f6a: helm values and templates for controlplane.affinity/dnsProxy
e5f6a7b: terraform module hooks and README
f6a7b8c: unit tests and integration harness for resolver emulation
review_thread: Oliver Chen: Please add a migration safety check to avoid changing subnet IDs during a routine upgrade — otherwise lgtm.
Priya Natarajan: Added a dry-run check and migration opt-in flag; updated CLI help text. PTAL.
María Rossi: Verified RBAC scope for the dns controller; left one small comment about retry backoff for resolver rule APIs.
Derek Patel: Ran the staging e2e; observed expected resolver entries and benign fallback behavior when the proxy is disabled. Approved.
source.merge_result: Merged via squash on 2026-02-24 by Priya Natarajan. Commit message consolidated allocator + dns proxy changes. No post-merge hotfix required. Changelog entry added under Unreleased.
source.notes_for_ops: Operator notes: when enabling strict affinity in a live cluster, run installer with --dry-run first to preview subnet reassignments. If dns-proxy-enabled=true is used across accounts, ensure the SUP-4421 cross-account resolver IAM role is provisioned. TTL for conditional forwarders defaults to 60s and can be tuned via controlplane.dnsProxy.ttl.
source.testing_summary: Unit: allocator heuristics covered with synthetic zone/capacity inputs. Integration: localstack-based resolver tests validated create/update/delete of conditional forward rules. Staging: deployed to two region staging clusters with cross-VPC peering; observed stable control-plane during simulated AZ drain.

source.title: split-airgap-values-and-delegated-init-wave-controller
source.author: Marta Liu
source.description: Motivation: air-gapped/private installs have several implicit assumptions in the existing values.yaml templates: image pre-pull behavior, init job sequencing, and hook ownership are entangled with a single values tree which makes upgrades and rollbacks fragile when offline registries or signing/verification steps are required. This PR separates the airgap-specific value overlays from the core init sequencing templates and introduces a delegated init-wave controller that can be toggled per-tenant. Summary of changes: 1) Add values schema: chart/values-airgap.schema.json (new overlay set) and update values.schema.json with a top-level 'airgap' overlay pointer. 2) Factor templates: chart/templates/init-waves-delegated.yaml (new controller-backed wave template), chart/templates/init-waves-legacy.yaml (backward-compatible path), chart/templates/airgap-prepull-daemonset.yaml (optional, gated by airgap.prepull.enabled). 3) Helm helpers: chart/templates/_helpers-airgap.tpl for imagePullSecrets and signed-verify hooks. 4) Introduce hook annotations and signer tolerations to support on-prem signing daemons without altering the main RBAC. 5) Migration: a migration script is included under tooling/migrations/airgap-values-migrate.sh to translate old values.airgap.presets into the new shape with safe defaults. 6) Add unit and cluster-level tests: tests/helm/airgap-values-compat.test and e2e/upgrades/airgap-rollback.test. Compatibility: default behaviour is unchanged unless clusters opt-in with values.airgap.enabled=true or by setting delegatedInit.enabled=true. Migration notes: clusters using the old airgap.presets should run the migration script before upgrading the chart; automated migration will be run for Dedicated installs during staging rollout; for on-prem customers we recommend running the script in a maintenance window. Rollback safety: the delegated controller introduces a feature-flag and a legacy-path so charts can be rolled back safely if the controller is not yet available. Testing: ran helm lint, chart-verify, unit tests, and multi-tenant e2e on a local airgapped kind cluster with image mirroring. Related: references ENG-2731 for phased rollouts and SUP-8942 for a customer support thread about failed airgap post-upgrade hooks. Checklist: - [x] values schema and examples - [x] migration helper script - [x] helm tests and e2e - [x] docs: docs/airgap/README.md updated with migration steps - [x] addrelease notes entry
commits: a1b2c3: factor airgap values into dedicated overlay and schema
d4e5f6: add delegated init-wave controller template and helper functions
f7g8h9: add migration script, tests, and docs
review_comments: Ethan Park (2026-02-16): This split looks sensible. Please add a migration step to the CI gating so Dedicated upgrades run the translator automatically.
Marta Liu (2026-02-16): Added a migration helper and a note; gating change added to staging pipeline in a follow-up commit.

Diego Marquez (2026-02-18): I see a potential RBAC gap for the delegated controller when running in restricted namespaces; please make sure the chart documents required roles and add a patch in templates/_rbac-delegated.yaml.
Marta Liu (2026-02-19): Added _rbac-delegated.yaml and tests to confirm role bindings are created only when delegatedInit.enabled=true.
Sofia Andersson (2026-02-21): Verified e2e failure earlier; after the RBAC fix and bumping the controller image the e2e passed. Approving.
CI Bot (2026-02-22): helm lint failed on line 42 of init-waves-delegated.yaml due to missing template helper; fixed in subsequent commit.
Ethan Park (2026-02-23): Approved after verifying migration script idempotency in a dry-run.
source.release_notes: Add optional airgap values overlay and delegated init-wave controller. Migration helper included to convert legacy airgap.presets to new schema. Default behaviour unchanged unless airgap.enabled or delegatedInit.enabled is set.

source.title: Introduce key-sink operator and upgrade-safe TLS signer orchestration for private installer
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.release_notes: Added a new key-sink operator and orchestrated TLS signer rotation hooks to make private installer upgrades safe for customers using external KMS/HSM. This includes Helm postinstall/predelete hooks and Terraform idempotency fixes.
source.description:
Problem: Upgrading the private installer in environments that use an external KMS/HSM and a cluster-based TLS signer often required manual steps: draining signer keys, resealing/unsealing Vault, and coordinating certificate signer swaps. This caused support churn for on-prem upgrades and blocked some air-gapped installs.

Goal: Introduce a light-weight 'key-sink' operator that can absorb signer key handoffs, provide a predictable handover API during chart upgrades, and add upgrade/rollback hooks (Helm + Terraform) so the installer can perform non-disruptive TLS signer rotations and Vault unseal orchestration. The implementation must be idempotent and safe to re-run for interrupted upgrades.

What this PR does: Adds a Kubernetes operator (key-sink) into the private-installer distribution that watches for SignerHandOff custom resources and atomically persists ephemeral signer material to a sealed secret and optionally to an external KMS-backed sink. Adds Helm lifecycle hooks (pre-upgrade hook to stage the handoff, post-upgrade hook to reconcile live signer, pre-delete hook to safely drain ephemeral keys) and Terraform module updates to support idempotent provisioning of the KMS sink IAM roles and HSM partition bindings. Also includes: integration test harness updates for on-prem flows, example upgrade playbook in docs, and a small migration job to reconcile existing Vault sealed secret names.

Design notes and motivations: The operator intentionally keeps the handoff API narrow: a SignerHandOff resource contains a stable logical name, rotation intent (boolean), and a reference to a target sink (sealedsecret or kms). The reconcile loop performs a compare-and-swap using server-side apply and a small sequencer annotation to avoid race conditions during parallel upgrades. We avoid storing unencrypted keys in-cluster unless sealedsecret is enabled in the customer's namespace. For HSM-backed sinks we defer to the external KMS bridge module (updated Terraform) and only store metadata in-cluster.

Backward compatibility and safety: The Helm chart adds the operator as an opt-in subchart (enabled by default for new installs). Existing clusters without the operator continue to work because the pre-upgrade hooks are no-ops when the operator is not present. All hooks are written to be re-entrant: they check leader annotations and the presence of a completed marker before performing destructive actions.

Testing and validation: Unit tests for the operator reconciler and acceptance tests in the perf-canary job (k8s integration profile) were added. E2E smoke tests simulate a signer rotation during an in-progress upgrade and verify zero downtime TLS handoff for a sample control-plane microservice. CI initially failed on a flaky e2e; I isolated the race and added a wait-with-timeout in the test harness. CI now passes.

Migration notes: A migration script (bin/migrate-existing-signer.sh) attempts to find legacy sealedsecret names and create SignerHandOff resources to allow a smooth transition; a manual step is documented for privileged installs where operator cannot access existing sealed secrets.

Why unique from prior work: Existing rotation automation focused on Vault auto-unseal, HSM keywraps, and sealedsecret choreography. This change introduces a focused Kubernetes operator for signer handoffs and explicit upgrade hooks that are agnostic to Vault vs. external KMS, enabling safer control-plane certificate rotations across both on-prem and VPC private deployments.
commits: a1c6f9b: add key-sink operator scaffold and CRD (pkg/operator, api/v1alpha1)
b4d12e3: helm: add key-sink subchart with hooks and templates for SignerHandOff CRs
c78d9f0: terraform: kms-sink module idempotency and role binding improvements
d35ac21: docs: upgrade-playbook.md and operator FAQ
e9f3b4a: test: integration and e2e signeroff scenario and CI fixes
f12e334: scripts: add migration helper and sanity checks
review_comments: Samir Zhou: Request changes - please add leader-election on the controller and a short explanation on how the sequence annotation prevents concurrent clobbering.
Priya Natarajan: Reply - added leader election and an inline comment in reconciler, and expanded design section in docs to describe sequencer behavior.
Elena Martinez: Request changes - Terraform role creation needs Least Privilege; reduce IAM permissions and document required permissions for offline installs.
Priya Natarajan: Reply - narrowed IAM policy scope and added an offline install doc block showing how to pre-seed the HSM partition binding; added unit tests for module input validation.
Jonah Park: Approved - tested chart upgrade against a local Kind cluster with a Vault dev instance; hooks behaved as expected and migration script recovered legacy sealed secrets.
CI Bot: e2e/perf-canary failed on first run due to timing race; rerun and passed after test harness fix.
source.notes: Operator is opt-in for existing installs via values.operator.enabled; default onboarding flow will enable on new private-installer deployments. Post-merge: run bin/migrate-existing-signer.sh in a maintenance window for clusters with legacy sealed secrets.
source.changelog_entry: Added key-sink operator and upgrade hooks to safely rotate TLS signers and improve KMS/HSM handoffs during private-installer upgrades.
manual_steps: If using external HSM, ensure the terraform kms-sink IAM role is provisioned before enabling the operator.
For air-gapped installs without SealSecret controller, run the migration script manually with credentials that can access existing sealed secrets.

source.title: HSM Shield operator: staged key promotion + TLS signer + graceful Helm upgrade hooks
source.author: Ava Chen
source.description:
Problem / Context: Customers running Private deployments in air-gapped or regulated datacenters require safe, auditable HSM key rotations and certificate replacements that can be performed with zero-downtime and an automated rollback path. Existing tooling handled key promotion but lacked an orchestrated, Helm-native flow for staged promotion, a local TLS signing shim for offline environments, and Terraform primitives to bind policy and grants consistently across upgrades.

This PR introduces the 'HSM Shield' operator and several installer surface changes to provide: staged key promotion (canary -> staged -> promoted) with automatic traffic fencing, a TLS signing adapter that can run in air-gapped clusters and perform CSR approvals via a controlled HSM signing path, Helm post-upgrade/rollback hooks to ensure safe cert churn during chart upgrades, and a small Terraform module to bind operator IAM/KMS grants as part of the installer.

What I changed (summary):
- Added a new Kubernetes operator: ops/hsm-shield-operator (controller + CRDs) implementing staged promotion FSM, audit annotations, and reconciliation backoff policies.
- Introduced a lightweight TLS signer sidecar (images/hsm-tls-signer) that validates CSRs and proxies signing requests to an HSM enclave over mTLS; supports offline signing via queued signed artifacts.
- Helm chart updates: templates/hsm-shield/*.yaml with post-upgrade hooks that quiesce traffic, rotate certs in a controlled sequence, and run a validation job before lifting the quiesce. Hooks are idempotent and safe to re-run.
- Terraform: modules/installer/kms-binding module to create consistent binding resources (policy attachments, grant objects) required by the operator in supported clouds. Includes tests for idempotency and drift detection.
- Migration script: scripts/migrate-sealed-secrets-to-protobuf.sh to transition sealed-secrets secrets to the new encrypted envelope format consumed by the operator.
- Docs: docs/private/hsm-shield-playbook.md with runbook instructions for canary promotion, rollback, and emergency key freeze.

Why this approach: The operator centralizes state (canary/promote/rollback) and emits audit-friendly annotations for KMS/HSM actions so customers can satisfy compliance requests. The TLS signer addresses air-gapped clusters by allowing sign operations to either happen synchronized with a physically-attached HSM or via an approved offline signing flow. Embedding Helm hooks lets cluster operators upgrade the installer without manual certificate choreography.

Testing and validation:
- Unit tests for operator FSM and reconcile loops included.
- Integration tests: e2e scenario that runs in CI using a software HSM shim (softHSM) to validate key promotion and TLS rotation flow. One e2e test is flaky under heavy load and was re-run in CI (now stabilized).
- Terraform acceptance tests validate binding creation and idempotency.

Rollout notes / migration:
- Operators will detect existing sealed-secrets payloads and create a migration job; the migration is opt-in by setting values.hsmShield.migrate=true in the values file.
- Default behavior is conservative: new operator will not auto-promote keys unless explicitly triggered by a PromotionRequest CR.

Checklist:
- [x] CRD definitions and RBAC
- [x] Operator reconcile + unit tests
- [x] TLS signer sidecar + image build scripts
- [x] Helm hooks and chart linting
- [x] Terraform binding module + acceptance tests
- [x] Migration script and docs

Related work: ENG-4127 (staged-rotation-spec), ENG-4189 (installer-terraform-binding), SUP-8421 incident follow-up automation.

Notes for reviewers: focus on reconcile idempotency, Helm hook safety (double-run behavior), and Terraform grant lifecycle. The operator logs include structured events to simplify incident replay.
commits: Initial operator scaffold and CRDs
Add staged promotion FSM and reconciliation logic
TLS signer sidecar implementation and softHSM adapter
Helm chart templates + post-upgrade hooks
Terraform module for KMS binding and tests
Migration script + docs and playbook
Fix flake in e2e promotion test (backoff tuning)
review_comments: Diego Morales: Can we make the promotion window configurable per-namespace? I want default tight windows for infra namespaces. (requested change)
Author (Ava Chen): Added values.hsmShield.promotionWindow and per-namespace override; updated docs to recommend 15m default for infra namespaces.

Priya Natarajan: Helm hooks look good but please ensure the upgrade hook is tolerant to partial failure (we should never leave pods permanently quiesced). Add a retry + backoff in the validation job. (requested change)
Author (Ava Chen): Validation job now runs with 5 retries and exponential backoff; hook marks completion using a ConfigMap lease to avoid races.

Samir Patel: Terraform module should not create cloud IAM service accounts automatically in air-gapped installs; suggest a flag to skip SA creation and only emit grant templates. (approved with comment)
Author (Ava Chen): Added skip_service_account_creation flag and documented manual steps in the playbook.

source.title: staged-egress-nat-allocator-and-conditional-dns-delegation-rollback
source.author: Ethan Park
source.description: Motivation: customer on-prem control planes need deterministic AZ-aware egress NAT allocation and an auditable, reversible handoff of Route53 private delegation for cross-account resolver setups. This PR introduces a Terraform module and installer hooks to provision a staged egress NAT allocator with zone affinity, ephemeral ENI support for control plane endpoints, and a conditional DNS delegation workflow that can be rolled back safely. Summary of changes: 1) terraform/modules/egress-nat-allocator: new module that computes AZ affinity, allocates NAT Gateways or ENI-based NAT proxies based on deployment flavor, and exposes a staged allocation plan for rollback. 2) terraform/modules/controlplane-subnet: add tags and ownership metadata to enable deterministic subnet selection by allocator. 3) installer/hooks/preflight and installer/hooks/post-rollout: add scripted handoff steps to create cross-account Route53 delegation records, sign delegation tokens, and optionally register fallback resolver rules. 4) helm/redwood-private/templates/daemonset-egress-proxy.yaml: add annotations for ephemeral-eni lifecycle hooks. 5) tooling/rollback/rollback-manager: add a lightweight Terraform wrapper that can revert staged NAT assignments and revoke delegation tokens in a controlled order. Checklist: [x] module implemented and unit-tested, [x] helm changes linted, [x] integration tests for staged rollout and rollback, [x] migration plan documented in release_notes. Migration notes: new TF state resources introduce a staged "allocation_plan" remote state key; operators must run `terraform apply -var=apply_stage=true` to commit the staged allocation. By default the installer runs in "staged" mode to allow verification before commit. Rollback instructions: use tooling/rollback/rollback-manager rollback --plan-id=<id> which will reverse ENI attachments, release allocated NAT IPs back to the pool, and remove delegation tokens while keeping controlplane subnets intact. Testing: ran full CI (terraform fmt, terraform validate, helm lint, unit tests) and a canary deployment in lab region eu-west-1. Observability: added metrics for allocation latency and delegation propagation in prometheus/metrics.yaml. Security: delegation tokens are KMS-encrypted and rotated on rollback. Performance: expected ~2x faster failover when using AZ-affine ENI proxies vs cold NAT Gateway recreation in multi-AZ failover scenarios. Related work: builds on ENG-1197 (subnet affinity tagging) and ENG-2589 (endpoint lifecycle hooks). Risks: cross-account IAM role propagation requires operator IAM permissions; documented preflight checks fail early if missing. This PR is intentionally conservative about automatic commit to avoid accidental DNS flip. Commits summary and changed files are provided below.
commits: a1f7c32: feat(egress-nat-allocator): initial module and plan output (terraform/modules/egress-nat-allocator)
b3d9f4e: chore(controlplane-subnet): add deterministic tags + unit tests
d45eb21: feat(installer-hooks): conditional dns delegation + rollback-manager tooling
review_comments: Maya Singh: Please add an explicit IAM permissions block in the docs for the cross-account delegation role; operators were surprised during canary. Ethan: Good catch, added docs and preflight check.
Julien Roy: The ENI detach path looked racy in the first patch, can you add an ExponentialBackoff on detach? Ethan: Added retry/backoff with idempotent checks in tooling/rollback/rollback-manager.
Priya Kapoor: CI failed once on terraform fmt, fixed formatting in module outputs.tf and re-ran tests.
Maya Singh: Approving after doc and backoff changes. Julien Roy: Approved. Priya Kapoor: Approved.
source.release_notes: Introduced staged egress NAT allocator with AZ affinity, conditional Route53 private DNS delegation with rollback tooling, installer preflight checks for cross-account IAM. Operators must run staged apply to commit allocations. See docs/private-deployments/egress-allocator.md for upgrade steps.
