What changed: - New module: terraform/modules/gpu-nodepool-stencil/ implementing deterministic naming strategy, nodepool fencing IAM templates, and optional network-anchor checkpointing hook. - New outputs and inputs to make module pluggable into existing private-installer stacks (vpc_id, subnet_tags, tenant_id, naming_seed). - Add tfstate-checkpoint script (scripts/tfstate_checkpoint.sh) used as a post-apply hook to write a minimal JSON checkpoint object to configured object store with network tag metadata. - Add migrator: scripts/tfstate_migrator_detname.go to compute deterministic names and optionally migrate state entries for existing installs (dry-run mode by default). - Add e2e-local test harness under test/integration/gpu-nodepool-stencil/ that runs a plan/apply against a local GCS-compatible backend (minio) to validate checkpoint creation and IAM binding lifecycle. - Documentation: docs/private-deploy/gpu-nodepool-stencil.md with examples and migration steps. - CI: terraform fmt/lint, unit tests for naming math, and an integration smoke test. Backwards compatibility & migration: The migrator is intentionally opt-in. The module has a toggle 'adopt_existing_names' and 'migrator_mode' with 'dry-run' by default; operators must run the migrator and confirm checkpoints before flipping adoption flags. Checklist: - [x] Terraform module implemented with full variable validation - [x] Post-apply checkpoint hook implemented and tested against minio - [x] Migrator included and documented - [x] Unit tests for naming algorithm - [x] Integration smoke test added - [x] Docs added and linked in private-installer README Testing & validation: Ran CI pipeline (format/lint/plan) — initially one formatting error, fixed in commit 3. Integration harness executed end-to-end on local minio and created checkpoint objects with expected metadata. Security & IAM: The module creates IAM role templates only; actual role assumption is gated by a 'lease' provider that issues ephemeral tokens (TTL configurable). The default behavior is conservative (no IAM creation unless 'enable_ephemeral_iam' is true). Migration notes: Run scripts/tfstate_migrator_detname.go --dry-run to preview changes, then create checkpoints for current state, then flip 'adopt_existing_names'. Related work: ENG-6342 (deterministic node ids), ENG-6401 (rollback checkpoints).

Existing modules create GPU nodepools with provider-generated names and rely on global tfstate only; this makes cross-account reconciliation and on-prem rollback brittle. source.title: Add break-glass flag for bundle verification bypass + explicit audit warning
source.author: Ethan Park
source.body:
Context: We now fail-closed on offline bundle signature/manifest verification during preflight. For a small set of air-gapped environments, we still need an emergency, explicitly-audited path to proceed when verification cannot be performed (e.g., corrupted trust store update package, temporary time source issues, or operational incident). This PR introduces a break-glass override flag that is policy-gated and leaves strong evidence (console warnings + local audit markers) when used.

What this PR does: Adds a break-glass path for preflight/install that allows skipping bundle signature verification only when an explicit local policy allows it. The override is intentionally noisy and requires operators to provide a reason + approval reference, and we emit audit events for both attempted and successful usage.

CLI / UX changes: - New flag: --break-glass-skip-bundle-verification (alias: --break-glass) - Required when break-glass is used: --break-glass-reason <string> and --break-glass-approval <string> (e.g., Jira SUP-31977 / internal incident ID / customer change ticket) - Optional: --break-glass-operator <string> (defaults to current OS user if resolvable) - When triggered, installer prints a multi-line WARNING banner before proceeding, including a summary of what checks are being bypassed, and paths to logs/support bundle. - Break-glass is blocked by default unless policy explicitly allows it.

Policy gating: Break-glass requires one of the following (in priority order): 1) /etc/redwood/installer/policy.yaml contains allow_break_glass: true 2) --policy-file <path> pointing to a policy yaml with allow_break_glass: true If neither is present/enabled, the installer fails with a clear error and does not proceed.

Audit markers / telemetry: - Emits local audit event: private_installer.bundle_verification.override_attempted (always when flags are supplied) - Emits local audit event: private_installer.bundle_verification.override_used (only if policy allows and we proceed) - Event includes: bundle_build_id (if readable), bundle_manifest_digest (if present), bundle_path, installer_version, reason, approval, operator, host_id (hashed), timestamp, and a boolean indicating whether verification was skipped vs succeeded. - Also writes an explicit marker into the install state record (state.json) so subsequent support bundles can see that the current install was performed under break-glass.

Safety rails: - Break-glass only affects the bundle signature verification step (manifest signature + trust store). Hash verification of the manifest file itself is not possible without a trusted signature, so we do not attempt partial verification that could be misconstrued as trustworthy. - We do NOT change any behavior around network access: in air-gapped mode, installer still will not attempt to fetch missing artifacts. - Break-glass is not available for the inspect command unless the same policy gate is satisfied, to reduce casual misuse.

Testing: - Unit tests for flag parsing, required reason/approval enforcement, and policy gate behavior. - Integration tests for: (a) break-glass blocked by default, (b) break-glass allowed with policy file, (c) audit event emitted and contains required fields, (d) install state marker set when used.

Files/areas changed (high level): - cmd/private-installer/main.go: wiring new flags and help text - internal/preflight/verify_bundle.go: add override path + structured errors - internal/policy/policy.go: read allow_break_glass from policy yaml (existing parser extended) - internal/audit/events.go + internal/audit/emitter.go: new event types + severity WARN mapping - internal/state/state.go: persist break-glass marker + reason/approval reference - docs/cli.md: document flags, example usage, and support expectations

Example output (trimmed): WARNING: BREAK-GLASS MODE ENABLED. Bundle signature verification has been SKIPPED. This increases supply-chain risk and must only be used with explicit approval. Reason: "Customer time source unavailable (air-gapped)" Approval: "SUP-31977" Audit event written to: /var/log/redwood-installer/audit.log

Rollout notes: - This is intended for emergency use only; normal installs should never use break-glass. - Security/GRC requested explicit evidence on usage; audit markers are designed to be SIEM-exportable in later work.

Follow-ups (not in this PR): - Wire the new audit event into the audit-log-exporter schema (tracked separately) - Add a support-bundle section that highlights break-glass usage prominently (partially addressed via state marker; formatting improvements later)

Checklist: - [x] Requires policy gate (default deny) - [x] Requires reason + approval reference - [x] Emits audit events for attempted/used - [x] Leaves local state marker - [x] Tests cover deny/allow paths - [x] Docs updated

Risk assessment: This change intentionally introduces a bypass, but it is tightly gated, noisy, and auditable. The default posture remains fail-closed. The primary risk is operator misuse; mitigations are (1) policy requirement, (2) required approval reference, (3) persistent audit evidence.

Refs: ENG-4824 (break-glass override design and policy), ENG-4823 (installer verification fail-closed), SUP-31977 (support process guidance request).
commits: add break-glass flags and validate required args
policy: add allow_break_glass gate + policy file loading
preflight: plumb override to bundle verification step
audit: add override_attempted/override_used events with WARN severity
state: persist break-glass marker + approval reference
tests: unit + integration coverage for break-glass behavior
docs: CLI usage and security notes
conversation: Marcus Lin (Security Eng): Can we ensure this is default-deny even if the flag is provided? I don't want a world where a customer can just pass --break-glass and proceed.
Ethan Park (Author): Yes — implemented as policy-gated. Without allow_break_glass: true in policy.yaml (or an explicit --policy-file), we error and exit before any install steps run.
Kevin Osei (Audit): Please include an event for attempted use even when blocked, and include approval reference + reason as required fields.

Ethan Park (Author): Done. Added override_attempted on flag detection (before policy decision) and override_used only when proceeding. Reason/approval are required; missing values fail fast with a dedicated error code.
Sean Gallagher (SRE): Make the console warning impossible to miss and ensure it ends up in logs for ticket attachments.
Ethan Park (Author): Added a banner printed to stderr and also logged to installer log; audit event goes to audit.log. Also persisted marker in state.json so support bundles can highlight it.
Dev Patel (Support): Please include an example that references SUP-* so the team knows what to put in the approval field.
Ethan Park (Author): Updated docs/cli.md with examples (SUP-31977 + internal incident).
Marcus Lin (Security Eng): LGTM with the gating + audit markers. Please keep this scoped to skipping signature verification only (no other checks).
Ethan Park (Author): Confirmed; only the signature verification step is bypassed; no network behavior changes.
release_notes: Private installer: added policy-gated break-glass override to skip offline bundle signature verification in emergencies; usage is explicitly warned and recorded in local audit logs.

source.title: Docs: update air-gapped installation flow for signed offline bundles
source.author: Kira Thompson
source.description:
Summary: Updates the in-repo installer documentation to reflect the new signed offline bundle workflow used for air-gapped installs. This PR is intentionally focused on README/docs content and examples (no behavior changes), and aligns terminology with recent installer work: bundle manifest, detached signature, trust store, preflight verification, downgrade prevention, and break-glass override logging.

Why: Pilot customers and Support/SEs were following the older “copy bundle + run install” flow and missing the required verification step. In addition, the new tooling (bundle inspect/verify) and the fail-closed behavior in preflight needs to be documented with the exact command lines and expected outputs so air-gapped operators can self-diagnose issues like hash mismatch, unknown key, and time skew.

What changed (docs only): 1) Rewrote the Air-gapped Installation section to make verification an explicit required step before install. 2) Added a “Bundle layout” explanation with examples (manifest + manifest.sig alongside artifacts). 3) Added a troubleshooting table mapping common error codes/messages to remediation steps and where logs are written. 4) Documented how to supply/refresh the trust store in air-gapped environments (including key rotation expectations). 5) Documented break-glass override flag usage as a last resort, including audit implications and required follow-up.

Key examples added: - Verify bundle signature and manifest before install: `rwi bundle inspect --bundle ./redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01.0.tgz --verify` - Run preflight in air-gapped mode (fails closed on verification): `rwi install preflight --air-gapped --bundle ./redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01.0.tgz` - Install from a verified bundle: `rwi install --air-gapped --bundle ./redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01.0.tgz` - Explicitly point to a trust store file (for rotation updates): `rwi install preflight --air-gapped --bundle ./bundle.tgz --trust-store ./trust/redwood-trust.json` - Break-glass (policy gated): `rwi install --air-gapped --bundle ./bundle.tgz --break-glass-allow-unsigned` (docs emphasize this emits warnings/audit markers and should only be used with approval)

Notes: - These docs assume the signed bundle layout introduced by recent work (detached signature file) and the preflight verification behavior from ENG-4823. - Time skew guidance is included (air-gapped environments frequently lack NTP); docs include a short note on diagnosing “not yet valid” failures and collecting the system clock/time source details for Support.

Testing: - `make lint` - `make test` - `make docs-check` (markdown link checker) - Local verification of rendered README code blocks + copy/paste command sanity check against current installer CLI help output.

Follow-ups: - The public docs site update is tracked separately in redwood-docs; this PR is specifically for in-repo installer docs that ship with the on-prem package.
commits: docs(readme): rework air-gapped install flow around signed bundles
docs: add bundle layout + manifest/signature examples
docs: add preflight/inspect/verify command snippets and expected output
docs: add troubleshooting table for common verification failures
docs: document trust store updates and key rotation expectations
docs: add break-glass guidance and required audit notes
chore: fix internal anchor links and consistency pass
changed_files: README.md (Air-gapped installation flow updated; new verification-first steps)
docs/security/offline-bundle-signing.md (new: trust store + key rotation operator notes)
docs/cli-reference.md (updated snippets for bundle inspect/preflight flags)
.github/workflows/docs-check.yml (minor: include docs/ in link check paths)
conversation: Elliot Price (review): The flow is much clearer now. Two nits: (1) call out that preflight is required and fails closed if verification fails; (2) add a short section on what files Support will ask for (manifest, signature, installer logs).

Kira Thompson (author): Updated the top of the air-gapped section to explicitly mark “Verify + Preflight” as required, and added a “What to collect for Support” bullet list including `install.log`, `verification.json` (if present), `manifest.json`, and `manifest.sig`.

Marcus Lin (review): Please tighten the break-glass wording. We should avoid implying this is a standard workaround; also require that the docs mention audit markers and that customers should remediate by restoring normal verification ASAP.

Kira Thompson (author): Updated break-glass section: emphasized last resort, approval required, emits explicit audit warnings, and includes follow-up steps (update trust store / re-transfer bundle) before next upgrade.

Dev Patel (review): Troubleshooting table is great. Can we add one row for the common “hash mismatch after transfer” scenario (zip/unzip tools, line ending corruption, partial copy) and recommend verifying checksums on the transport media?

Kira Thompson (author): Added row for hash mismatch after transfer with remediation steps: re-copy using a binary-safe tool, re-verify the bundle archive checksum on both ends, avoid auto-extract/repack, and confirm filesystem free space.
Ethan Park (review): CLI flags look right, but the docs mention `--trust-store` in install. Confirm it works in both `preflight` and `install` subcommands and that the name matches help output.

Kira Thompson (author): Confirmed against current CLI help; `--trust-store` is supported for both `install preflight` and `install`. Updated the examples to match exact flag names and ordering shown by `rwi install --help`.
Elliot Price (final): LGTM after the anchor/link fixes. Thanks for keeping the language consistent with the docs site.
Merge: Squash merged by Ethan Park.
release_notes: Docs: Updated air-gapped installation instructions to require signed bundle verification and preflight prior to install.
Docs: Added troubleshooting guidance for signature verification failures (unknown key, hash mismatch, time skew) and trust store updates.

What changed (docs only): 1) Rewrote the Air-gapped Installation section to make verification an explicit required step before install. 2) Added a “Bundle layout” explanation with examples (manifest + manifest.sig alongside artifacts). 3) Added a troubleshooting table mapping common error codes/messages to remediation steps and where logs are written. 4) Documented how to supply/refresh the trust store in air-gapped environments (including key rotation expectations). 5) Documented break-glass override flag usage as a last resort, including audit implications and required follow-up.

Key examples added: - Verify bundle signature and manifest before install: `rwi bundle inspect --bundle ./redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01.0.tgz --verify` - Run preflight in air-gapped mode (fails closed on verification): `rwi install preflight --air-gapped --bundle ./redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01.0.tgz` - Install from a verified bundle: `rwi install --air-gapped --bundle ./redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01.0.tgz` - Explicitly point to a trust store file (for rotation updates): `rwi install preflight --air-gapped --bundle ./bundle.tgz --trust-store ./trust/redwood-trust.json` - Break-glass (policy gated): `rwi install --air-gapped --bundle ./bundle.tgz --break-glass-allow-unsigned` (docs emphasize this emits warnings/audit markers and should only be used with approval)

Notes: - These docs assume the signed bundle layout introduced by recent work (detached signature file) and the preflight verification behavior from ENG-4823. - Time skew guidance is included (air-gapped environments frequently lack NTP); docs include a short note on diagnosing “not yet valid” failures and collecting the system clock/time source details for Support.

Testing: - `make lint` - `make test` - `make docs-check` (markdown link checker) - Local verification of rendered README code blocks + copy/paste command sanity check against current installer CLI help output.

Follow-ups: - The public docs site update is tracked separately in redwood-docs; this PR is specifically for in-repo installer docs that ship with the on-prem package.

source.title: local-registry-fast-sync-and-rollback-snapshots
source.author: Samira Patel
source.release_notes: Adds a fast-sync local registry seeder and snapshot-backed rollback mechanism for on-prem/offline installs. Includes a Terraform module, Helm chart hooks, e2e tests, and operator guide updates.
source.description: Motivation: Private on-prem installs frequently hit long bootstrap windows when seeding large OCI registries over slow links. This PR introduces a delta-aware fast-sync engine plus snapshot-based rollback anchors so customers can perform atomic upgrades and quickly revert to a known registry state in air-gapped or VPC environments. What changed: 1) new Terraform module redwood.local_registry that orchestrates an initial seeding plan and creates a signed snapshot manifest, 2) a sync agent (installer/bin/registry-sync) that performs checksum-driven delta pulls, resumes interrupted transfers, and applies layer-level parallelism tuned for low-latency paper networks, 3) snapshot manifest format and a lightweight anchor scheme (snap-<epoch>-<seq>) stored in the registry under a signed tag, 4) Helm chart additions: registry-mirror-init sidecar, offline-values configmap templating and a preflight hook to validate snapshot compatibility, 5) upgrade/rollback tools: installer/bin/rollback-to-snapshot that verifies chain integrity before promoting a snapshot atomically, 6) docs and runbooks under docs/installer/reg-sync.md and runbooks/rollback-playbook.md, 7) tests: e2e against a local OCI mirror and unit tests for manifest signing/verify. Rollback approach: snapshots are created before any upgrade step, indexed by monotonically increasing sequence and a signed digest. Rollback validates that the registry contains all referenced layers and performs an atomic tag swap using a temporary tag promotion flow to avoid partial reads. Backwards compatibility: the manifest schema v1 is backward compatible with our existing sealed bundle indexer; the Terraform module offers both a 'seed' and 'resume' mode. Testing and validation: ran offline e2e in a simulated 10Mbps link with large model blobs; median bootstrap time reduced 4.2x versus baseline, resume tests passed on network interruption, and rollback exercise completed within expected latency budget. Security considerations: manifests are signed with customer KMS keys when available; sidecar runs as non-root. Migration notes: existing install workflows that rely on the previous registry seeding script can opt-in to 'fast_sync=true' in the installer config. Checklist: - [x] Terraform module + docs - [x] sync agent binary + unit tests - [x] Helm chart hooks and templates - [x] rollback tool + runbook - [x] e2e validation and CI integration - [x] docs and example values How to validate locally: follow docs/installer/local-fast-sync.md to bring up a miniregistry, run terraform apply in examples/registry-seed, then run installer/bin/registry-sync --plan examples/plan.json. Related: follows discussions in INFRA-234 and reuses part of the delta-indexing code introduced in ENG-42099.
commits: feat(installer): add redwood.local_registry terraform module and example plan
feat(sync): implement registry-sync agent with delta resume and parallel layer fetch
feat(helm): add registry-mirror-init sidecar and offline-values templating
feat(rollback): add rollback-to-snapshot tool and promotion flow
test(e2e): add fast-sync e2e harness and link-throttle simulator
docs: add reg-sync and rollback runbooks and example configs
chore: update CI to run registry-sync unit tests in containerized job
review_conversation: Riley Chen: Nice approach; two quick asks: please add a small integration test that simulates an interrupted transfer and also document KMS key rotation implications.
Samira Patel: Added an e2e interrupt test and a KMS rotation section in reg-sync.md, clarified how signed manifests are reissued during rotation.
Mateo Alvarez: The Terraform module looks good but can we avoid setting default wide-open ACLs on the bootstrap bucket? Suggest making ACL explicit and documenting least-privilege pattern.
Samira Patel: Updated module to require an explicit 'bootstrap_bucket_acl' variable and added an example policy in examples/terraform/README.md.
Priya Nandan: Verified helm templates with chart lint and dry-run; minor nit about indentation in preflight-hook, added LGTM after patch.
Samira Patel: Fixed indentation, addressed comment; requesting squash-merge when CI green.
source.merge_commit_message: redwood(private-installer): add fast-sync local registry seeder + snapshot rollback tools (ENG-54321)
source.notes: This PR seeds a new optional flow. Operations should flip fast_sync to true for large installs after validation. Follow the runbook for rollback drills before enabling automatic upgrades.

source.title: Prevent offline bundle downgrade using signed manifest version + local install state
source.author: Jordan Lee
source.release_notes: Private installer now blocks installing an offline bundle with a signed manifest version lower than the last successfully installed version on the same host (downgrade protection).
source.body:
## Summary
This PR adds downgrade protection for Redwood Private offline bundles by enforcing monotonic install versions based on signed manifest metadata and a local install state record. After signature verification succeeds, the installer compares the bundle's signed version against the last successfully installed version recorded on disk; if the incoming bundle is older, preflight fails closed with an actionable error.

## Why
Air-gapped customers frequently move bundles around via removable media. Without downgrade protection, an attacker (or an operational mistake) can replay an older-but-valid signed bundle to reintroduce known vulnerabilities or regress critical fixes. Because our threat model includes replay/downgrade as a realistic risk in offline workflows, we need the installer to defend against accidental or malicious rollbacks by default.

## Design
- New signed manifest fields consumed by the installer:
  - `bundleVersion` (string): semantic-ish version used for ordering (e.g., `2026.01.0`, `2026.01.1-rc.2`). This field is already covered by the manifest signature.
  - `buildId` (string): opaque build identifier for support correlation (not used for ordering, but recorded).
- New local install state record:
  - Path: `/var/lib/redwood-private-installer/install-state.json` (Linux default). The path is configurable via `--state-dir` for non-standard environments.
  - Contents recorded after successful install only: `lastInstalledVersion`, `lastInstalledBuildId`, `lastVerifiedManifestSha256`, `lastVerifiedKeyId`, `installedAt`.
  - File permissions: created with `0600` where possible; directory with `0700`.
- Enforcement:
  - Preflight flow becomes:
    1) Load + verify manifest signature (existing behavior from PR-24844).
    2) Parse `bundleVersion` from signed manifest.
    3) Load install state (if present).
    4) If `bundleVersion` < `lastInstalledVersion`, fail preflight with `RPI-VER-009` (DowngradeBlocked).
  - If no install state exists (fresh install), no downgrade check is applied.

## Version ordering
We need stable ordering that behaves sensibly across our release trains:
- Supports `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` plus optional prerelease tags (e.g., `-rc.1`).
- Treats prerelease as lower than the corresponding stable release: `2026.01.0-rc.2` < `2026.01.0`.
- If parsing fails for either side, we fail closed with `RPI-VER-010` (UnparseableVersion) and instruct the operator to use a newer bundle/installer or contact Support.

## Operator experience
Example error:
`ERROR RPI-VER-009: Downgrade blocked. Bundle version 2026.01.0 is older than last installed version 2026.01.2 on this host. Downgrades are disabled by default to prevent replay of older signed bundles. If you are attempting an approved rollback, contact Redwood Support for the documented break-glass procedure.`

Notes:
- This PR does NOT add the break-glass override flag; that is tracked separately to ensure we get security policy + audit semantics right. For now, rollback requires following the documented support procedure (manual remediation) and is intentionally not self-serve.

## Test plan
- Unit tests for version parsing and ordering (stable vs prerelease, invalid strings, edge cases).
- Installer integration test: install bundle A (writes state), attempt bundle older than A (blocked), attempt newer bundle (allowed), ensure state updates only after successful install.
- Manual: verified on an offline VM with existing signed bundle artifacts; verified error messages are surfaced in both `preflight` and full `install` path.

## Security notes
- The install-state file is not a security root of trust; it is an enforcement mechanism to prevent accidental/operational downgrade. An attacker with root access could tamper with it; that threat is out of scope for offline bundle verification controls and is covered by host hardening expectations.
- The ordering decision is based on signed manifest metadata, so tampering with the bundle version requires invalidating the signature.

## Follow-ups
- Add audited break-glass override flag (ENG-4824) with explicit local audit events.
- Document rollback procedure in air-gapped guide + runbooks.
- Consider pinning minimum allowed version per cluster in a cluster-scoped state record (future).

Checklist
- [x] Added tests
- [x] Updated error taxonomy (RPI-VER-009/010)
- [x] Verified state file permissions
- [x] Validated behavior on fresh install vs upgrade

Refs
- Linear: ENG-4827 (tamper/replay/downgrade), ENG-4823 (verification fail-closed)
- Threat model: Offline bundle signing (replay/downgrade mitigation)
commits: installer/state: introduce InstallState record + filesystem helpers
preflight: enforce monotonic bundleVersion after signature verification
versioning: add SemVer-ish parser with prerelease handling
errors: add RPI-VER-009 DowngradeBlocked + RPI-VER-010 UnparseableVersion
tests: unit tests for version ordering + integration test for downgrade block
review_conversation: Marcus Lin (Security): We should be explicit that the local state file is not a trust anchor. If an attacker has root, they can remove it and downgrade anyway; but this still blocks operator mistakes. Also: are we writing state before install completes? Jordan Lee: Agree; added a note in the PR and ensured state is persisted only after the final "install succeeded" step. I also tightened permissions (0600) and made directory creation best-effort with clear logging.

Connor O'Brien (Release Eng): How does this interact with RC builds? We sometimes need to go from rc -> stable or stable -> rc during validation. Jordan Lee: RC is allowed to upgrade to stable (rc < stable). Stable -> rc will be blocked since it's a downgrade by definition; this is intended. If Release needs a special workflow on CI hosts, we can run with a dedicated state-dir in CI or use ephemeral runners. Added a note to the integration test docs.

Ethan Park (Installer): Please make sure the error surfaced in `install` path is the same as `preflight`. Also, can we include the last installed buildId in the message for Support? Jordan Lee: Updated error formatting to include last buildId when present and ensured both codepaths call the same preflight verifier function.

Sean Gallagher (SRE): Where does the state live on systems that don't have /var/lib? We see some hardened images. Jordan Lee: Added `--state-dir` and documented it. Default remains /var/lib for Linux; if it cannot be created, we fail closed because downgrade protection can’t be guaranteed (consistent with fail-closed posture).

source.title: Add SBOM pointer + sha256 to offline bundle manifest and verify offline
source.author: Jordan Lee
source.release_notes: - Offline bundle manifest can now include SBOM metadata (path/URI + sha256). - Installer preflight verifies SBOM hash offline when present; fails closed on mismatch/missing file in offline mode.
source.description: Context: Air-gapped customers are asking for a verifiable SBOM as part of the offline bundle so they can do internal intake (security scanning / procurement) without relying on external links. We already sign the manifest and verify artifact hashes; this PR extends the manifest to reference the SBOM artifact(s) and verifies the SBOM file hash offline during preflight. Summary of changes: (1) Manifest schema: adds an optional `sbom` entry with fields for `uri` (relative path in bundle or file:// URI), `sha256` (hex), and `format` (e.g., spdx-json, cyclonedx-json). The SBOM entry is included in the signed manifest payload, so tampering is detected via signature verification and also via explicit hash verification. (2) Bundle layout convention: recommends placing SBOMs under `sbom/` in the bundle root (e.g., `sbom/redwood-private-<version>.spdx.json`). The manifest stores the relative path (preferred) or file:// URI for non-standard layouts. We explicitly do NOT support fetching SBOMs over the network during install; if the manifest uses http(s) and `--air-gapped` is enabled, preflight fails with an actionable error. (3) Installer preflight: adds a verification step `verifySbom()` executed after manifest signature verification and before chart/image checks. Behavior: if `sbom` is present, the installer resolves the SBOM file path, computes sha256, compares to manifest, and fails closed on mismatch; if the SBOM file is missing, it fails in air-gapped mode and warns (non-fatal) in connected mode (to preserve forward-compat while we migrate internal bundle builders). If `sbom` is not present, preflight proceeds with a single warning line noting SBOM verification was skipped. (4) Tests: adds unit tests for schema parse + canonicalization (sbom fields included), and preflight verification tests covering: missing sbom file, mismatched hash, and a valid SBOM hash. Adds a regression test to ensure we never attempt network access in air-gapped mode even if a URI is mistakenly set to https://. (5) UX: introduces specific error codes/messages used by Support: `BUNDLE_SBOM_NOT_FOUND`, `BUNDLE_SBOM_HASH_MISMATCH`, `BUNDLE_SBOM_URI_UNSUPPORTED_OFFLINE`. Includes remediation tips (verify transfer integrity, re-extract bundle, confirm you have the correct SBOM file that matches the bundle build id). Implementation notes: The manifest remains canonicalized via our existing stable JSON encoder; the new `sbom` fields are treated as normal keys and appear deterministically. We intentionally kept this additive (no schema version bump) to avoid bricking older bundles; older installer versions will ignore the new fields, but this installer will verify them when present. Follow-ups (out of scope here): (a) make SBOM presence required for GA bundles (gated in release tooling), (b) allow multiple SBOMs (per-component) if compliance asks; current schema supports a single top-level SBOM pointer which is sufficient for pilot customers. Security considerations: This reduces supply-chain risk for offline deployments by ensuring the SBOM itself is integrity-protected and tied to the exact bundle contents. It also prevents a class of “swap SBOM during transfer” issues that came up during customer security reviews. Related: ENG-4821 (manifest schema/canonicalization), ENG-4823 (installer verification), ENG-4829 (audit events for verification outcomes; this PR does not add new audit events beyond existing preflight result logging).
commits: manifest: add optional sbom metadata (uri, format, sha256)
installer: verify sbom sha256 during preflight (offline-safe)
tests: add sbom hash mismatch + missing file coverage
docs: update offline bundle layout notes for sbom/

source.review_conversation: Marcus Lin (Security) requested we fail closed on SBOM hash mismatch and ensure no network fetch occurs in air-gapped mode, even if a URI is provided. Author updated the resolver to reject http(s) URIs when `--air-gapped` is true and added a regression test that asserts no outbound attempts. Kevin Osei asked whether the SBOM hash verification result would be visible in support bundles/audit logs; Jordan noted this PR only adds preflight errors and warnings and referenced ENG-4829/PR-25011 for wiring bundle metadata into support bundles. Ethan Park requested clearer messaging for the “SBOM missing in connected mode” case to avoid confusing customers; author adjusted the warning to include next steps (rebuild bundle / contact support) and added the exact path that was attempted. Connor O'Brien asked about schema compatibility with older installers and release tooling; author confirmed the field is optional and that release tooling can start populating it immediately without needing a manifest version bump, and added a small note to docs/offline-bundles.md.
source.merge_outcome: Merged via squash into main after approvals (Marcus Lin, Ethan Park) and CI green. Squash commit message: "Add SBOM pointer + sha256 to offline bundle manifest"

title: Add SBOM pointer + sha256 to offline bundle manifest and verify offline
preview: Context: Air-gapped customers are asking for a verifiable SBOM as part of the offline bundle so they can do internal intake (security scanning / procurement) without relying on external links. We already sign the manifest and verify artifact hashes; this PR extends the manifest to reference the SBOM artifact(s) and verifies the SBOM file hash offline during preflight. Summary of changes: (1) Manifest schema: adds an optional `sbom` entry with fields for `uri` (relative path in bundle or file:// URI), `sha256` (hex), and `format` (e.g., spdx-json, cyclonedx-json). The SBOM entry is included in the signed manifest payload, so tampering is detected via signature verification and also via explicit hash verification. (2) Bundle layout convention: recommends placing SBOMs under `sbom/` in the bundle root (e.g., `sbom/redwood-private-<version>.spdx.json`). The manifest stores the relative path (preferred) or file:// URI for non-standard layouts. We explicitly do NOT support fetching SBOMs over the network during install; if the manifest uses http(s) and `--air-gapped` is enabled, preflight fails with an actionable error. (3) Installer preflight: adds a verification step `verifySbom()` executed after manifest signature verification and before chart/image checks. Behavior: if `sbom` is present, the installer resolves the SBOM file path, computes sha256, compares to manifest, and fails closed on mismatch; if the SBOM file is missing, it fails in air-gapped mode and warns (non-fatal) in connected mode (to preserve forward-compat while we migrate internal bundle builders). If `sbom` is not present, preflight proceeds with a single warning line noting SBOM verification was skipped. (4) Tests: adds unit tests for schema parse + canonicalization (sbom fields included), and preflight verification tests covering: missing sbom file, mismatched hash, and a valid SBOM hash. Adds a regression test to ensure we never attempt network access in air-gapped mode even if a URI is mistakenly set to https://. (5) UX: introduces specific error codes/messages used by Support: `BUNDLE_SBOM_NOT_FOUND`, `BUNDLE_SBOM_HASH_MISMATCH`, `BUNDLE_SBOM_URI_UNSUPPORTED_OFFLINE`. Includes remediation tips (verify transfer integrity, re-extract bundle, confirm you have the correct SBOM file that matches the bundle build id). Implementation notes: The manifest remains canonicalized via our existing stable JSON encoder; the new `sbom` fields are treated as normal keys and appear deterministically. We intentionally kept this additive (no schema version bump) to avoid bricking older bundles; older installer versions will ignore the new fields, but this installer will verify them when present. Follow-ups (out of scope here): (a) make SBOM presence required for GA bundles (gated in release tooling), (b) allow multiple SBOMs (per-component) if compliance asks; current schema supports a single top-level SBOM pointer which is sufficient for pilot customers. Security considerations: This reduces supply-chain risk for offline deployments by ensuring the SBOM itself is integrity-protected and tied to the exact bundle contents. It also prevents a class of “swap SBOM during transfer” issues that came up during customer security reviews. Related: ENG-4821 (manifest schema/canonicalization), ENG-4823 (installer verification), ENG-4829 (audit events for verification outcomes; this PR does not add new audit events beyond existing preflight result logging).

source.title: Wire signed offline bundle metadata into generated support bundle
source.author: Ethan Park
source.body: Context: With signed offline bundles, Support/SEs frequently need to answer "did verification run" and "what exactly was verified" from a support bundle alone (esp. in air-gapped environments where customers share a tarball via offline transport). Today the support bundle includes installer logs and config, but it does not summarize the offline bundle’s manifest/signature state, leading to longer back-and-forth (SUP-31844) and mis-triage when transport corrupts artifacts (SUP-31892). This PR adds a structured summary file and a small amount of diagnostic output so that a support bundle can stand alone for first-pass triage without exposing sensitive customer data. What this changes: (1) Adds a new support bundle attachment: support/bundle-verification.json containing the last-known offline bundle verification result, plus key metadata pulled from the signed manifest and verifier. (2) Includes the raw verification error code + remediation hint (not the full stack trace) to align with the installer’s verification error taxonomy. (3) Captures bundle identity fields needed to correlate to release artifacts: bundle_build_id, bundle_version, manifest_digest_sha256, signature_digest_sha256, signer_key_id, trust_store_version, and installer_version. (4) Adds a "verification_context" block that records whether verification ran during preflight, whether break-glass override was used, and any detected time-skew handling path (without recording the host clock). Safety/redaction: no file paths outside the bundle root are recorded; no customer tokens/URLs are included; the trust store content is not dumped, only the configured trust store version/id and the selected key id. Implementation notes: the support bundle generator now consumes the same in-memory VerificationReport object returned by preflight (or loads the persisted report if present) and writes a stable JSON file using canonical key ordering for diffs. If verification has never been run, the support bundle records status=unknown and includes next steps. Testing: unit tests cover "success", "hash mismatch", "unknown key", "not yet valid" and "break-glass" cases; integration test verifies support bundle output is produced in air-gapped mode. Release note: none (internal supportability improvement), but docs/runbook owners have been notified to reference the new file in triage steps.
commits: supportbundle: add bundle-verification.json attachment and writer
preflight: persist verification report for support bundle reuse
verifier: expose signer key id + signature digest in VerificationReport
tests: add fixtures for verification failure mapping + support bundle output
docs: update internal SUPPORT.md triage section for signed bundles
conversation: Dev Patel (review): Can we make sure Support has a single place to look without parsing logs? Suggest a top-level "status" plus "reason_code" and a short "next_steps" string.
Ethan Park (author): Added top-level status + reason_code + next_steps. Mapped next_steps from existing error taxonomy so it stays consistent with CLI output.
Logan Wright (review): Please confirm we’re not emitting anything that could be considered customer environment PII (hostnames, internal mount paths). Also avoid including the entire trust store.

Ethan Park (author): Confirmed: no hostnames, no absolute paths, no trust store dump. Only trust_store_version + selected signer_key_id. Added a unit test asserting no "path:" keys and no "trusted_keys" array is emitted.

Marcus Lin (review): From a security perspective, include manifest digest + signature digest so we can detect replay/tamper from the support bundle. Also ensure the file is clearly marked as derived from verification, not a new source of truth.

Ethan Park (author): Added manifest_digest_sha256 + signature_digest_sha256. File header comment is not possible in JSON, but added "generated_by" and "source" fields (e.g., source=preflight_verification_report) and documented in SUPPORT.md.
Owen Phillips (review): In SUP-31892 we saw customers using zip/unzip that altered files. Can we surface "bundle_path_transport_warning" when we detect common corruption patterns?

Ethan Park (author): I didn’t add heuristics here (risk of false positives), but I did include the normalized error mapping and next_steps that explicitly calls out "re-transfer bundle, avoid tools that modify permissions/line endings" for hash mismatch cases.
Dev Patel (approval): This will materially reduce first-response time. Approved once CI is green.
Logan Wright (approval): Looks good; thanks for keeping the redaction tight.
Marcus Lin (approval): Approved; security requirements met.
Ethan Park (author): Re-ran integration tests after addressing a flaky temp-dir assertion; all green.

source.title: Improve offline bundle verification error messages + remediation hints
source.author: Ethan Park
source.release_notes: Installer preflight now emits actionable verification failures (error codes, likely cause, remediation, log paths). No change to verification semantics; still fails closed by default.
source.description:
Context: With signed offline bundles, verification failures are expected to be one of the top support drivers during pilot. Today, failures often surface as generic crypto/library errors with no guidance on what to do next, and logs are not consistently referenced in the CLI output. This PR keeps fail-closed behavior unchanged, but makes the UX/support story significantly better.

What this changes: - Introduces a structured error taxonomy for bundle verification with stable error codes (RV-VERIFY-0xxx). - Maps common failure modes to a short 'what happened' + 'what to do next' remediation block, including where to find logs. - Adds a single-line summary suitable for copy/paste into tickets, plus a verbose output behind --verbose. - Normalizes log locations across Linux packages and containerized installer runs, and prints the resolved log path in the error footer. - Adds a 'transport integrity' remediation hint for hash mismatches seen in SUP-31892 (zip/unzip tooling, line-ending conversions, partial copies). - Adds time-skew specific messaging (not-yet-valid / expired) including suggested checks for air-gapped time sources.

Non-goals: - No change to verification policy (still blocks install on invalid/untrusted signatures). - No change to signature format / manifest schema (handled in other PRs). - No break-glass flag in this PR (tracked separately).

Testing: - Added unit tests for error mapping and formatting. - Updated integration tests to assert error codes + presence of remediation hints. - Manually validated output on Ubuntu 22.04 and RHEL 8 with tampered manifest + unknown key cases.

Docs: This PR updates the in-repo installer help text for the new error codes and log path footer. The full customer-facing docs update is tracked in ENG-4850.

Related PRs: - pr-24844-verify-bundle-signature-during-preflight - pr-24859-add-trust-store-and-key-rollover-support
commits: installer/verify: add VerificationErrorCode enum + classification helpers
cli/output: render remediation blocks and resolved log path footer
preflight: map crypto/x509 errors to stable RV-VERIFY codes
tests: assert codes + hints for tamper/unknown-key/time-skew cases
docs: update --help and troubleshooting pointers
changed_files: cmd/redwood-installer/main.go (wiring for verbose vs concise formatting)
docs/troubleshooting.md (minimal updates; links to ENG-4850 docs work)
source.conversation:
Marcus Lin (review): The mapping from x509 errors to our codes looks good. Please make sure we never print raw certificate subjects/serials by default (avoid leaking customer environment details into screenshots/tickets). Also, suggest adding an explicit note that verification failures are non-retriable unless the bundle/trust store changes.

Ethan Park (author): Good call. I moved cert detail printing behind --verbose and redacted serial/subject in the default path. Also added a 'Retry only after…' line to the remediation templates (bundle re-copy, trust store update, clock fix).

Dev Patel (review): Love the copy/paste line. Can we standardize the last line so Support can grep it in support bundles? Something like 'redwood-private-installer: RV-VERIFY-0012'. Also include the log file path *and* the support bundle path if generated.

Ethan Park (author): Added a stable footer: 'redwood-private-installer: <CODE> (see <log path>)'. For support bundle path, we only have it after bundle generation; I added a hint: 'If you generated a support bundle, include support-bundle.tgz'. PR-25011 will wire verification status into the bundle automatically.

Owen Phillips (review): For hash mismatch remediation, please explicitly warn about 'text mode' FTP / CRLF conversion and using 'unzip -aa'. We saw exactly that in SUP-31892.

Ethan Park (author): Updated the RV-VERIFY-0021 remediation block with concrete do/don't examples and added a link target to docs/troubleshooting.md section.

Kira Thompson (review): Can we keep wording consistent with docs ("offline bundle", "manifest", "detached signature")? Also suggest changing 'signature invalid' to 'signature verification failed' (more precise).

Ethan Park (author): Updated terminology to match our docs draft and made the message text more precise.

Sahana Iyer (review): CI looks fine. One nit: error code ordering in errors.go is out of sequence and will be painful to maintain. Please sort by numeric code and add a brief comment on stability guarantees.

Ethan Park (author): Done. Sorted codes, added comment: codes are stable across minor versions and should not be reused.

Marcus Lin (final approval): Approved after redaction changes + code stability comment. Please ensure we track adding these codes to the external docs FAQ (ENG-4850).

Dev Patel (final approval): Approved. This will reduce back-and-forth on pilot tickets.
source.merge_outcome: Merged via squash; commit message: 'Improve offline bundle verification error messages + remediation hints (#24873)'

source.title: Add integration tests for tampered offline bundles (fail closed)
source.author: Ethan Park
source.description: Context: As part of offline bundle signing + verification, we need high-signal integration tests that simulate real customer failure modes (tampering during transfer, swapped artifacts, partial copies) and ensure the installer preflight fails closed with actionable errors. This PR adds an integration test suite that generates/uses a minimal signed test bundle fixture and then applies targeted corruption steps: (1) modified manifest contents (JSON canonicalization changes + byte edits), (2) swapped artifact file while leaving manifest unchanged, (3) truncated bundle archive (simulating partial rsync/usb copy), and (4) missing signature file for detached layout. Each scenario asserts that `installer preflight` exits non-zero, emits the expected error code, and does not proceed to any install step. The suite also verifies that the error taxonomy remains stable (Support can map failures) and that logs include the verification reason without dumping sensitive bundle contents. Notes: The tests intentionally avoid any network usage and use a temporary isolated work dir to ensure they run in CI with outbound disabled. Related: ENG-4827, ENG-4823. Support context: SUP-31892 (hash mismatch after transfer) informed the truncated/corrupted archive coverage. Checklist: - [x] Add tamper fixtures + helpers - [x] Add integration coverage for manifest edit / artifact swap / archive truncation - [x] Assert fail-closed behavior (no install) - [x] Validate error codes + log redaction - [x] Run tests under `NO_NETWORK=1` in CI - [x] Document how to re-generate fixtures locally
commits: tests(integration): add offline bundle tamper harness and fixtures
tests(integration): cover manifest modification failure case
tests(integration): cover swapped artifact hash mismatch failure case
tests(integration): cover truncated archive and missing signature cases
ci: run offline bundle integration suite with outbound disabled
changed_areas: internal/testutil/offlinebundle: new helpers for bundle creation/copy/corruption
tests/integration/offline_bundle_verification_test.go: new integration test cases and assertions
cmd/installer/preflight: minor refactor to make error codes/assertions testable (no behavior change)
.github/workflows/ci.yml: add job for tamper integration tests with NO_NETWORK=1
docs/testing.md: instructions to run integration tests + regenerate fixtures
source.notable_snippet: Example test case added (abridged): `t.Run("fails on swapped artifact", func(t *testing.T) { bundle := fixture.SignedBundle(t) tampered := bundle.CopyToTemp(t) tampered.SwapFile("images/runtime-layer.tar", "testdata/alt-runtime-layer.tar") out, err := runPreflight(t, tampered.Path) require.Error(t, err) require.Contains(t, out, "ERR_BUNDLE_HASH_MISMATCH") require.Contains(t, out, "expected sha256") require.NotContains(t, out, "BEGIN PRIVATE KEY") })`
source.ci_summary: CI: ✅ build (linux/amd64), ✅ unit tests, ✅ lint, ✅ integration: offline bundle verification (NO_NETWORK=1), ✅ smoke: installer preflight matrix
review_conversation: Marcus Lin: Can we ensure the truncated-archive case doesn’t accidentally fall back to a best-effort unzip and continue? I want an explicit assert that no install step is invoked.

Ethan Park (author): Yep — added a sentinel file check + assertion that `install.plan` is never created and that we exit before any unpack-to-cache happens. Also asserting the error code is ERR_BUNDLE_READ_FAILURE.
Sahana Iyer: CI runtime looks like it jumped +6 minutes. Any way to keep this from becoming flaky? Also please confirm fixtures don’t rely on the signing service/HSM emulator.

Ethan Park (author): Reduced fixture size (single tiny chart + minimal image tar), and moved signing to a local test keypair committed under `testdata/keys` (public key only in repo; private key generated during test in temp dir). Net runtime +1m40s on my run. No HSM dependency.
Connor O'Brien: Please make the error text match the docs taxonomy we’re standardizing (ERR_BUNDLE_SIGNATURE_INVALID vs ERR_SIGNATURE_INVALID). I don’t want tests to lock in the wrong string.
Jordan Lee: +1. Prefer asserting on the structured code, not the full message. The message can change for UX.

Ethan Park (author): Updated assertions: tests check for `code=ERR_BUNDLE_SIGNATURE_INVALID` and only spot-check one human message line (not the full blob). Also aligned the code naming with the latest preflight error enum.
Sean Gallagher: For the swapped artifact test, can we simulate a realistic customer scenario (same filename, different bytes) rather than delete/rename?
Ethan Park (author): Done — now writes alternate bytes to the same path so it presents exactly like a silent corruption during offline transfer.
source.ci_failures_and_reruns: Initial CI run failed in `integration: offline bundle verification` due to a fixture path assumption on macOS runner (path separator). Fixed by using filepath.Join + normalized temp dir handling. Reran CI: green.
source.release_notes: Internal-only: strengthens supply-chain verification by adding integration coverage for common tampering/transfer corruption scenarios; installer is now continuously validated to fail closed on these conditions.

text: Context: As part of offline bundle signing + verification, we need high-signal integration tests that simulate real customer failure modes (tampering during transfer, swapped artifacts, partial copies) and ensure the installer preflight fails closed with actionable errors. This PR adds an integration test suite that generates/uses a minimal signed test bundle fixture and then applies targeted corruption steps: (1) modified manifest contents (JSON canonicalization changes + byte edits), (2) swapped artifact file while leaving manifest unchanged, (3) truncated bundle archive (simulating partial rsync/usb copy), and (4) missing signature file for detached layout. Each scenario asserts that `installer preflight` exits non-zero, emits the expected error code, and does not proceed to any install step. The suite also verifies that the error taxonomy remains stable (Support can map failures) and that logs include the verification reason without dumping sensitive bundle contents. Notes: The tests intentionally avoid any network usage and use a temporary isolated work dir to ensure they run in CI with outbound disabled. Related: ENG-4827, ENG-4823. Support context: SUP-31892 (hash mismatch after transfer) informed the truncated/corrupted archive coverage. Checklist: - [x] Add tamper fixtures + helpers - [x] Add integration coverage for manifest edit / artifact swap / archive truncation - [x] Assert fail-closed behavior (no install) - [x] Validate error codes + log redaction - [x] Run tests under `NO_NETWORK=1` in CI - [x] Document how to re-generate fixtures locally

source.title: Refactor bundle download-disabled paths (air-gapped mode never fetches missing artifacts)
source.author: Ethan Park
source.description:
Context: We’ve had multiple pilot/poc environments where outbound egress is hard-disabled (or DNS is blackholed), but the installer still *attempts* to fetch “helpful” missing artifacts (chart tarballs, index.yaml, image manifests) when an offline bundle is incomplete or the user points at the wrong bundle path. In air-gapped installs this produces long timeouts and confusing errors, and more importantly violates the expectation that air-gapped mode never reaches out to the network.

This PR hardens the download-disabled paths by (1) centralizing network policy enforcement and (2) making missing-offline-artifact handling fail-fast with actionable remediation.

What changed: - Introduced a single NetworkPolicy gate (AllowOutbound|DenyOutbound) wired into all downloader call sites. If DenyOutbound is active, any attempt to invoke a downloader returns a typed error immediately (no retries, no DNS resolution, no proxy evaluation). - Refactored bundle resolution so that “offline bundle present?” and “artifact present in bundle?” checks happen *before* we construct remote URLs. - Removed legacy fallback behavior that would try to pull Helm chart tarballs from the release CDN when the offline bundle chart directory was missing. - Updated preflight to explicitly enumerate required artifacts for the chosen install mode and fail closed when any are missing. Error includes: missing path(s), expected bundle layout hint, and a reminder to run `redwood-private-installer bundle inspect` (added in pr-24888) for a complete report.

Behavioral changes (user-visible): - `--air-gapped` (and config `network.outbound=disabled`) now guarantees zero outbound requests from the installer process. If the bundle is incomplete, the install stops during preflight with a deterministic error. - Timeout-style failures (waiting on dead DNS / blocked egress) are replaced with immediate, actionable failures.

Implementation notes: - Added `internal/netpolicy` with a lightweight interface (`AssertOutboundAllowed(ctx, op)`) that is called in: chart loader, image list resolver, optional SBOM pointer fetcher, and telemetry exporter init. - Error taxonomy: new error code `RPIE-NET-001` for outbound attempts while outbound is disabled, and `RPIE-BUNDLE-014` for missing required offline artifact. Both are surfaced in console output and logs.

Testing: - Added unit tests for netpolicy gating (ensures no downloader stubs are invoked). - Added integration test covering the regression: air-gapped install with missing `charts/` directory must fail fast and must not call the HTTP transport (uses a test HTTP round-tripper that panics if invoked). - Reproduced SUP-31892 scenario locally using a zip/unzip workflow that drops symlinks; preflight now reports the specific missing paths.

Rollout/risk: - Change is scoped to air-gapped/outbound-disabled modes; default (online) behavior still supports downloading optional artifacts where configured. - This is a compatibility tightening: installs that previously “sort of worked” by downloading missing charts will now fail unless the bundle is complete. This is desired for Private/air-gapped and aligns with the signed bundle work.

Docs follow-up: Separate PR will update the in-repo README to mention the stricter behavior and point users to `bundle inspect` output for support cases.

References: ENG-4823 (installer preflight verification and fail-closed), ENG-4827 (integration tests tamper/replay/downgrade), SUP-31892 (hash mismatch after transfer / incomplete bundle symptoms).
commits: installer: add netpolicy gate and typed errors
offline: refactor bundle artifact resolution to fail-fast
preflight: enumerate required artifacts for air-gapped installs
tests: add integration test ensuring no outbound HTTP calls
logging: improve remediation hints for missing offline artifacts
changed_areas: internal/bundle (artifact presence checks; removal of remote fallback)
review_conversation: Sean Gallagher (review): Can we guarantee we don’t even attempt DNS resolution in air-gapped mode? The previous bug was painful because it looked like a hang and triggered watchdogs.

Ethan Park (author): Yes—netpolicy asserts before URL construction / transport creation. Integration test uses a round-tripper that panics if invoked. Also removed the legacy code path that built CDN URLs as a fallback.
Marcus Lin (review): Please ensure error messages don’t suggest using break-glass flags here. Air-gapped mode should fail closed and the remediation should be “fix the bundle / path.”

Ethan Park (author): Updated remediation text to only reference `bundle inspect` + bundle layout; no mention of break-glass. Break-glass remains signature-specific and policy gated in a separate path.
Sahana Iyer (review): Any impact on CI release jobs that run with outbound disabled for hermetic builds?

Ethan Park (author): Those jobs set outbound disabled but don’t rely on downloaders at runtime; they pass. Added a small note in comments and ensured default remains outbound allowed unless explicitly disabled.
Colin O'Donnell (review): Can the preflight error include the exact relative bundle paths? That would save us time during pilots.
Ethan Park (author): Added missing path list in error + log and included an example expected layout. Also `bundle inspect` prints a concise diff of required vs present.
source.release_notes: - Air-gapped / outbound-disabled installs now fail fast when required offline artifacts are missing, and the installer will not attempt any outbound downloads in these modes.
source.breaking_change: No (tightened behavior only when outbound is explicitly disabled / air-gapped mode is selected).

source.title: Emit local audit/telemetry events for offline bundle verification outcomes (with redaction)
source.author: Logan Wright
source.description:
Summary: This PR emits local audit/telemetry events during offline bundle signature+manifest verification. Events cover both success and failure paths with a stable schema and an explicit redaction policy suitable for air-gapped/regulated environments. The installer continues to make no outbound network calls; events are written locally and can later be exported via the existing audit-log-exporter integration (when present) or collected via support bundles.

Motivation: Support and customer security teams asked for an auditable record of verification outcomes to answer: (1) did the installer verify the bundle? (2) why did it fail? (3) which trust root was used? and (4) did anyone use override/break-glass. Prior behavior only printed CLI output and wrote unstructured logs, which is hard to correlate and insufficient for security reviews.

Behavior: - On successful verification, emit event bundle.verify.success. - On failure, emit bundle.verify.failure with reason_code mapped from the existing error taxonomy (e.g., HASH_MISMATCH, SIGNATURE_INVALID, UNKNOWN_KEY, CERT_NOT_YET_VALID, CERT_EXPIRED, MANIFEST_PARSE_ERROR, DOWNGRADE_BLOCKED, BUNDLE_LAYOUT_INVALID). - If break-glass override is used (introduced in a separate PR), emit bundle.verify.override_used with an explicit warning flag and the provided justification string (redacted by default).

Schema (v1): event_name, event_version=1, timestamp, installer_version, bundle_path (optional/redacted), bundle_build_id (if present in manifest), bundle_version, manifest_digest_sha256, signature_digest_sha256, signing_key_id (KID / fingerprint), trust_store_id, verification_mode (preflight|install), outcome (success|failure|override), reason_code (on failure), error_message (redacted by default), host_facts (optional: os, arch), and correlation_id.

Redaction: Added config keys to control the payload. Defaults are conservative for regulated installs: - redact.bundle_path=true (default) - redact.error_message=true (default; reason_code is preserved) - redact.justification=true (default) - include.host_facts=false (default) Operators can opt-in to more detail via installer config or CLI flags. The intent is to preserve a useful security signal without leaking filesystem layout, customer project names, or operator-entered text into audit logs.

Storage & retention: Events are written to the local audit sink as JSON lines at /var/lib/redwood-private-installer/audit/events.jsonl (path configurable). Rotation uses size-based rolling with max file count; defaults mirror our other local audit logs. Nothing is transmitted.

Support bundle: When support bundle generation is enabled, the last N verification events are included (subject to redaction settings) to help triage common failures like hash mismatches after offline transfer.

Testing: - Unit tests for schema generation, redaction behavior, and reason_code mapping. - Integration test updated to assert a failure produces a bundle.verify.failure event with the correct reason_code. - Golden event fixtures to keep schema stable.

Docs: Added a short section in installer docs: Where to find verification audit events, how to change redaction levels, and what fields are expected.

Release note: Private/on-prem installs now produce local audit events for offline bundle verification (success/failure) with configurable redaction.

Commits: 1) installer/audit: introduce VerificationEvent schema + sink plumbing 2) installer/verify: map verification errors to stable reason_code enum 3) installer/config: add redaction config + CLI flags 4) tests: add fixtures and schema regression tests 5) docs: describe local audit events and paths

Changed areas (high level): - pkg/verify/*: emit verification outcome events at key decision points - pkg/audit/*: local JSONL sink + rotation + support bundle hook - pkg/config/*: redaction and sink configuration - docs/*: audit/telemetry section

Illustrative event (redacted defaults): {"event_name":"bundle.verify.failure","event_version":1,"timestamp":"2025-12-21T19:22:11Z","installer_version":"0.18.0","bundle_build_id":"priv-2026.01.0+build.1842","bundle_version":"2026.01.0","manifest_digest_sha256":"…","signature_digest_sha256":"…","signing_key_id":"RW-REL-01","trust_store_id":"embedded-v2","verification_mode":"preflight","outcome":"failure","reason_code":"UNKNOWN_KEY","correlation_id":"f7b1…"}

Review notes / discussion: - Kevin Osei requested we keep error_message redacted by default and ensure reason_code coverage is complete; addressed by adding an enum exhaustiveness test. - Marcus Lin asked for explicit override event semantics and to ensure override events can’t be mistaken for success; addressed by outcome=override and separate event_name bundle.verify.override_used. - Allison Grant requested alignment with company audit naming conventions and event_versioning; updated event_name prefix to bundle.verify.* and added event_version field. - Ethan Park requested we don’t emit duplicate events in both preflight and install for the same run; implemented correlation_id reuse and a guard so install only emits if preflight didn’t already emit for the same verification pass.

PR conversation (abridged): - Kevin Osei (review): “Please confirm default redaction is conservative (no paths, no free-form strings). Also can we guarantee reason_code is stable for exporter mappings?” - Logan Wright (author): “Yes; defaults redact bundle_path/error_message/justification. reason_code is a fixed enum with a regression test + fixture.” - Marcus Lin (review): “Override usage must be auditable and visually distinct; do not emit success when override is used.” - Logan Wright: “Added bundle.verify.override_used and outcome=override. Also added warning=true in payload.” - Allison Grant (review): “Event names should follow <domain>.<action>.<result> and include event_version.” - Logan Wright: “Renamed + added event_version=1; updated fixtures.”

CI: - build-linux-amd64: pass - unit-tests: pass - integration-tests-offline: pass (1 retry due to flaky temp dir perms) - lint: pass

Merge: Squash merged into main. Follow-ups: (1) audit-log-exporter schema mapping lives in audit-log-exporter PR-3112; (2) ensure break-glass override emits justification only when redact.justification=false and with an explicit operator confirmation prompt (tracked in ENG-4824).

Merge: Squash merged into main. Follow-ups: (1) audit-log-exporter schema mapping lives in audit-log-exporter PR-3112; (2) ensure break-glass override emits justification only when redact.justification=false and with an explicit operator confirmation prompt (tracked in ENG-4824).

source.title: Fix air-gapped time skew causing "certificate not yet valid" during bundle signature verification
source.author: Ethan Park
source.description:
Context: Several pilot/on-prem installs in fully air-gapped environments hit preflight failures when verifying signed offline bundles with errors like `x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid` (typically "not yet valid"). Root cause was cluster/installer host clocks being behind (RTC drift, no NTP, staged VM templates). Because signature verification is time-bound (cert validity + signature timestamp), the verifier correctly failed closed, but the UX was misleading and the remediation path was unclear.

What this PR changes:
- Improves detection and messaging for the specific "not yet valid" time-skew case: we now surface the current system time, the signing cert `NotBefore` value, and the computed skew, plus a clear remediation checklist (set clock / enable NTP source / validate VM template time).
- Adds a small skew tolerance window (default 10 minutes) to account for minor clock drift without weakening the threat model for real replay/downgrade cases. This is intentionally small and only affects the `NotBefore` boundary check.
- Adds an explicit diagnostic-only override to run verification with a caller-provided time, so Support/SE can confirm "this is just time skew" without telling the customer to bypass verification. New flag: `--verify-time-override RFC3339` (preflight + inspect only). When used, we emit a local audit marker and print a warning that this does not change the required production behavior.
- Updates docs/help output for air-gapped environments with guidance on time sources (e.g., offline NTP appliances, hypervisor time sync, manual clock setting procedures) and common pitfalls.

Security stance:
- Default behavior remains fail-closed.
- The override is opt-in, diagnostic-only, and does not enable continuing install when verification fails for other reasons (unknown key, hash mismatch, expired cert, invalid signature). It is intended to help triage/support and to reduce "false incident" escalations.

Testing:
- Unit tests for parsing/validation of RFC3339 override values and skew calculations.
- Integration test coverage for the not-yet-valid path to ensure we provide actionable remediation steps and correct audit markers.

Follow-ups:
- The broader time-skew guidance is tracked under ENG-4850 (docs/runbooks).
commits: installer/verify: split x509 verification errors into typed reasons
installer/preflight: add time skew helper + improved remediation output
cli: add --verify-time-override (RFC3339) to preflight/inspect commands
security/audit: emit local audit marker when verify-time-override is used
tests: add coverage for not-yet-valid + skew tolerance behavior
docs: update air-gapped troubleshooting section for clock/NTP issues
source.ci_summary: Buildkite: build (pass), unit tests (pass), integration tests (pass), lint (pass). Rerun: integration tests initially failed due to brittle time-based assertion; updated test to use fixed clock.
review_conversation: Marcus Lin (Security Eng): The skew tolerance window worries me a bit. Can you justify 10 minutes and ensure it only impacts NotBefore checks (not NotAfter / signature timestamp validation)? Also, I want the override to be explicitly labeled diagnostic and to log locally when used.

Ethan Park (Author): Agreed. Tolerance is only applied when `now < notBefore` and `notBefore-now <= tolerance`. Not applied to NotAfter, signature validation, hash checks, or key trust decisions. Added `AUDIT_BUNDLE_VERIFY_TIME_OVERRIDE_USED` event when the override flag is set and warning text in CLI output. I also constrained override to preflight/inspect only (no install path).

Sean Gallagher (SRE): Can we include the system time and the NotBefore time in the error output? This will save multiple back-and-forths with customers. Also please include a one-liner about hypervisor time sync when running on VMs.
Ethan Park (Author): Added. Error now prints `SystemTime`, `CertNotBefore`, and `SkewSeconds`. Added VM template/hypervisor sync note to troubleshooting doc and CLI help snippet.
Sahana Iyer (Build/CI): The integration test is flaky because it relies on wall clock. Please refactor to use the injected clock helper.
Ethan Park (Author): Done. Tests now use a fixed clock and validate message fragments + reason codes instead of exact timestamps.

Connor O'Brien (Release Eng): Please add a short changelog line for the next Private installer cut so Release notes don't miss this. Also confirm the override doesn't create a supportable "bypass" story.

Ethan Park (Author): Added changelog entry. The override is framed as diagnostics only and still requires verification to pass before install can proceed; it just changes the evaluation time for troubleshooting.
Marcus Lin (Security Eng): Approved with the guardrails. Please ensure the docs call out that customers should not rely on override for production installs.
Ethan Park (Author): Docs updated with explicit warning + recommended time sync options.
source.release_notes: Private installer: improved error messages and troubleshooting for air-gapped signature verification failures caused by clock skew; added diagnostic-only verify-time override for Support/SE triage.

source.title: Support detached signature layout (manifest.json + manifest.sig) for offline bundles
source.author: Ethan Park
source.description:
Context: As part of offline bundle signing for Redwood Private, we need a stable on-disk layout that works well with standard file transfer tooling (USB, secure courier, malware scanning gateways) and doesn’t require rewriting the manifest to “embed” signature metadata. This PR adds first-class support for detached signatures and documents the expected bundle layout.

What’s changing: The installer now accepts either (a) detached signature layout: <bundle>/manifest.json + <bundle>/manifest.sig, or (b) the legacy/early layout where the signature is embedded in a single envelope file (supported for backwards compatibility during pilot). The verification preflight resolves the signature file by convention (manifest.sig alongside manifest.json) and fails closed if a signature is required but missing/unreadable.

Bundle layout (new recommended): - bundle-root/ - manifest.json (canonical JSON) - manifest.sig (detached signature over canonical bytes of manifest.json) - artifacts/ (charts, images index, runtime/model artifacts, etc.) - sbom/ (optional, referenced by manifest)

Implementation notes: - Verification flow now loads manifest.json, canonicalizes it using the same rules as the signer (stable key ordering + UTF-8, no trailing whitespace), then verifies manifest.sig against those canonical bytes. - The trust store selection logic is unchanged; this PR only changes how the signature is located and the bytes it covers. - Error messages were updated to be explicit about expected paths (e.g. "expected signature file at <bundle>/manifest.sig"). - Added unit + integration tests for: missing signature file, swapped signature file, signature present but for different manifest bytes, and “both present” behavior (prefer detached signature if both embedded envelope and manifest.sig exist).

Why detached signatures: - Avoids mutating the manifest after it’s generated (less room for drift and easier deterministic builds). - Plays nicer with customers that require separate signature artifacts for scanning/attestation workflows. - Makes it easier to inspect bundles with common tooling (cat/grep/jq) without needing signature envelope parsing.

Security considerations: - Signature verification remains mandatory in air-gapped mode when signing is enabled (default for signed-bundle-capable installer builds). - We still fail closed on verification errors; break-glass behavior is not introduced here (tracked separately).

Docs: Included README updates for the offline bundle directory layout and examples of how Support/SE should describe the required files during bundle transfer validation.

Test plan: - unit: go test ./... - integration: make test-offline-bundle-verify (covers detached signature happy path + negative cases) - manual: generated a sample bundle from signing pipeline output, removed manifest.sig to confirm failure is clear/actionable.

Follow-ups: - Update the docs site to match the detached signature layout as the primary example (DevEx PR will reference this). - Align bundle generation tooling to always emit manifest.sig (some earlier pilot tooling still wrote the envelope file only).
commits: installer/verify: add detached signature resolver (manifest.sig) and prefer detached when both exist
bundle/manifest: canonicalization helper extracted and reused by verifier
tests: add integration coverage for missing/swapped signature files
docs: document offline bundle layout and verification expectations
review_conversation: Jordan Lee: Can we be explicit about which file is signed? Some tooling signs the raw file bytes, others sign a canonical form. I want to avoid ambiguous verification across OSes.

Ethan Park (author): Good call — verification now signs/verifies canonical bytes (stable key ordering, UTF-8) and the docs call that out. Added a test where manifest.json is semantically same but different formatting to ensure it still verifies.
Marcus Lin: What’s the behavior if manifest.sig exists but manifest.json is missing, or if the signature file is unreadable due to permissions?

Ethan Park (author): Preflight errors with OFFLINE_BUNDLE_SIGNATURE_MISSING / OFFLINE_BUNDLE_SIGNATURE_UNREADABLE (respectively) and blocks install. Error message includes expected paths and suggests re-copying the bundle and checking permissions.
Sahana Iyer: Please make sure we don’t break earlier pilot bundles. Are we still accepting the envelope layout?
Ethan Park (author): Yes — we accept the legacy envelope file for now. If both are present, we prefer detached and log that choice (at debug) to help Support.
Dev Patel: Can you include a one-liner Support can tell customers for “what files do I need to see in the bundle root”?
Ethan Park (author): Added to docs/offline-bundles.md under “Quick sanity check”.
source.merge_outcome: Merged via squash; delete head branch after merge.
